I will now sharpen each.

---

# 2. Lower actual directions

The key distinction is:

\[
\text{formal lower carrier} \neq \text{actual-source tangent}.
\]

The formal carrier \(e_7\) exists because D4 has a clean matrix

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

But \(e_7\) enters \(B_Y\) only if an actual arithmetic variation has nonzero \(\ell_7\)-coordinate.

So the lower actual-source test is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0
\quad\text{and}\quad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0?
}
\]

A sufficient theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\text{ contains no free/local corrected-package carrier coordinate.}
}
\]

If actual-source data is generated only by EF/gamma/tail/chart parameters, then lower actual directions are source-linear or background-origin. They should not carry either:

\[
[\mathsf C_7]
\]

or the primitive

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\,12)
\]

\(Y12\)-signature.

Thus expected:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

If instead the model admits a genuine local lower package coordinate, then:

- if it is \(e_7\)-type, rank two collapses to rank one;
- if it is \(e_Y\)-type vertical, \(Y12\) dies locally;
- if it has two slopes, \(Y12\) dies locally.

\boxed{

}

# 3. \(E_2^Y\) local tangent

For rank two, graph-compatible \(E_2^Y\) is **not** enough. We need the local fiber component to be zero:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}

A fixed \(E_2^Y\) basepoint is safe:

\[
E_2^Y=\eta_{\fixed}e_Y,
\]

because it is not a tangent. It does not enter \(B_Y\).

A finite coherent amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
\]

is also not a local fiber tangent if its coefficients are global/block-coherent rather than atom-local. It belongs to the amplitude-Schur layer.

The rank-two blocker is only a **local derivative** of \(E_2^Y\):

\[
D_\theta E_2^Y
\]

with nonzero projection to either \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\).

So define the coefficient map

\[
c_E(\theta;p)
=
\frac{\Pi_YE_{\model}(\theta;p)}{y_Y(p)}.

Rank-two requires

\[
\boxed{
D_\theta c_E=0
\quad\text{for all actual local tangent parameters }\theta,
}
\]

at least in the primitive \(Y12\) fiber.

If

\[
D_\theta c_E\neq0
\]

but the coefficient is global/finite coherent, move it to amplitude Schur. If it is atom-local, rank-two fails and possibly the rank-one route dies too.

---

# 4. Labelled-realization local mismatch

The labelled/raw discrepancy is

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_{\norm}+R_{\whit}+R_{\proj}+R_{\trunc}+R_{\chart}+R_{\tail}.
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

\[

=
\Pi_Y
\left(

-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right),
\]

plus any

\[
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
\]

term if \(L\) is nonlinear, and the corresponding same-point whitening defect.

Rank two requires this commutator to be:

\[
\boxed{
0
}
\]

in the local fiber, not merely graph-like.

If \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is finite coherent, it contributes to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

and rank two remains possible at the local layer but needs vector amplitude Schur.

If \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is locally free, rank two fails.

So the exact routing is:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=
0
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
B_Y\text{ unaffected};
\]

\[
\mathfrak C_Y\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
amplitude\ Schur;
\]

\[
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ locally free}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
rank\text{-}two\ route\ fails.
\]

---

# 5. Nuisance quotient

Rank two requires the nuisance quotient not to touch the live pair:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently, \(e_7,e_Y\) must survive with a well-conditioned live Gram:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

If nuisance kills \(e_7\), rank two drops at least to rank one.

If nuisance kills \(e_Y\), the \(Y12\) route dies.

If nuisance identifies \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\), the two-coordinate fiber collapses.

This is not arithmetic absorption; it is quotient overcollapse unless justified by a real actual-source model relation.

---

# 6. Rank-two theorem

The rank-two theorem can now be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Local Rank-Two \(Y12\)-D4 Theorem.}
}
\]

Assume:

1. live pair survives:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A};
\]

2. no actual-source local fiber motion:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0;
\]

3. no local \(E_2^Y\) fiber tangent:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0;
\]

4. no local labelled-realization fiber mismatch:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}=0;
\]

5. no nuisance action on the live fiber:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}
\right)=2.
\]

D4 yields the vector wall

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

With

\[
|c_7|,|\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A},\qquad |\beta_9|\le Q^A,
\]

we get

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
}
\]

---

# 7. Why this is much stronger than rank one

The rank-one wall is

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

Its failure creates a scalar ODE trap.

The rank-two wall fails only if both

\[
\Gamma_G\approx0
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}\approx0.
\]

This is a codimension-two event and should be controlled by raw D4 anisotropy, not moving-line finite type.

Thus rank two bypasses:

\[
\rho_\Gamma\text{ honesty},
\]

\[
moving\text{-}line\ sublevel,
\]

\[
forced\ ODE\ trap,
\]

unless a vector amplitude model absorbs \(F_{2Y}\).

---

# 8. Remaining vector amplitude layer

Even with \(B_Y=0\), coherent amplitude spaces may remain:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Then the needed theorem is vector amplitude Schur:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
}
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}=0/\text{fixed},
\]

rank two gives a pointwise vector wall.

If finite coherent, use block Schur.

If locally free, rank two fails at amplitude level.

---

# 9. Current best route

The current best route is therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first prove }B_Y=0.
}
\]

Concretely, prove:

\[
\ell_7(U)=\ell_9^Y(U)=0
\]

for:

\[
U\in
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual},
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local},
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local},
\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

If all pass, use rank-two D4.

If one graph-like \(s_7\)-direction appears, descend to rank one.

If vertical/rank-two actual model appears, the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 10. Final current status

The strongest present hypothesis is:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
}
\]

under a fixed-target, EF-generated actual-source manifold with no free package-coordinate directions.

The most serious unresolved blockers are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\text{ contains actual }C_7\text{ motion?}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

\[

}
\]

If these are all zero, rank-two D4 should become the primary downstream route.

# 1. The live \(C_7\) carrier is not a source jet

The key distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(v)\neq [z^7]v.
}
\]

The covector \(\ell_7\) does not read an arbitrary order-seven Taylor coefficient. It reads the live carrier class

\[
[\mathsf C_7]
\]

in the realized singular quotient.

Thus an actual EF variation may have many order-seven local Taylor terms while still satisfying

\[
\ell_7(v)=0.
\]

This is the main reason rank two remains plausible.

---

# 2. Source-linear actual parameters should be killed by \(\ell_7\)

Let

\[
v=\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

for

\[
\theta\in\{m,\lambda,\mathrm{phase},\mathrm{chart},\mathrm{normalization},\mathrm{background}/\gamma\}.
\]

Such \(v\) is source-linear:

\[
v
=
\sum_n
\left(
\partial_\theta a_n+i a_n(\partial_\theta\tau_n)x
\right)e^{i\tau_nx}
+
\text{gamma/background/chart terms}.
\]

Its origin labels are:

\[
\mathrm{EF\ linear},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background},
\quad
\mathrm{chart}.
\]

The live \(C_7\) carrier is a D4 carrier class, not an arbitrary source-linear EF atom direction. So the expected signature separation is

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
}
\]

for all ordinary fixed-target actual parameters.

This proves

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=0
}
\]

provided the actual-source parameter list does not include free local corrected-package coordinates.

---

# 3. Fixed-\(\alpha\) second curvature should also be killed

For actual second curvature,

\[
v=\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can},
\]

the term is quadratic in parameters, but not a primitive D4 carrier. Schematically,

\[
\partial_i\partial_j(a_ne^{i\tau_nx})
=
P_{ij}(x,\tau_n,\partial\tau_n,\partial a_n)\,e^{i\tau_nx}.
\]

It remains EF-linear in source origin. Therefore it should not hit the live \(C_7\) carrier coordinate:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This is especially clean in the fixed-\(\alpha\) setting. If \(\alpha\) is only a proof-thickening variable for O2/O3, it should not be included in \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\). If \(\alpha\) is promoted to an actual-source deformation, this vanishing has to be re-run.

---

# 4. The only serious actual \(C_7\)-source is a package-coordinate leak

The only way I currently see for

\[
\ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})\neq0
\]

is if the actual-source model has admitted some local corrected-package coordinate as an actual source parameter.

That would mean a legitimate actual variation

\[

satisfies

\[
\left.\frac{d}{d\tau}\right|_{\tau=0}u(\tau)
=
e_7+\cdots,
\]

or at least

\[

\]

But this would be a severe over-enlargement of the actual-source manifold. The actual source should be generated by EF/gamma/tail data and fixed-target parameters, not by free local D4 carrier coordinates.

So the correct guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Actual-source manifolds may not include free corrected-package carrier coordinates.}
}
\]

Under that guardrail,

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. What about formal lower coherence?

The formal lower connection

\[
e_7\mapsto \rho_Ye_Y
\]

does not contradict this.

It says:

\[
\boxed{
\text{if actual \(s_7\)-motion exists, then its \(Y12\) continuation is graph-like.}
}
\]

It does not say actual \(s_7\)-motion exists.

Thus, if

\[
\ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

then the graph line

\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

does not enter \(B_Y\), and rank two remains available.

This is the central correction from the latest prompt: formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection is conditional, not an automatic quotient direction. 

---

# 6. Strong rank-two local theorem

The strongest local theorem now looks like this:

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

}
\]

\[

\]

for every local actual/nuisance generator \(U\).

Then D4 remains rank two.

---

# 1. Candidate master lemma

The remaining \(B_Y=0\) blockers are:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\qquad
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local},
\qquad
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local},
\qquad
\mathcal N_{\sing}.

A sufficient structural lemma is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{EF-generated actual-source separation.}
}
\]

Every actual-source/nuisance/local object is generated by EF/gamma/tail/chart/normalization data and by fixed canonical completions, while the live fiber coordinates \(s_7,s_9^Y\) detect corrected-package carrier classes. Therefore

\[
\ell_7(U)=\ell_9^Y(U)=0
\]

for every actual-source/nuisance/local generator \(U\), unless the model explicitly admits a free corrected-package carrier coordinate.

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right)=0.
}

\[
\boxed{
\text{EF-generated source directions}
\quad\perp\quad
\text{corrected-package live carrier directions}.
}
\]

---

# 2. Lower actual directions

The most dangerous remaining term is

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}.
\]

But this space should contain **actual lower source directions**, not formal D4 carrier variables.

\[
\mathrm{EF\ linear},\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background},\quad
\mathrm{chart},\quad
\mathrm{tail}.
\]

They do not have live corrected-package carrier labels:

\[
[\mathsf C_7],
\qquad
[\mathsf C_{Y12}].

Thus the desired vanishing is:

\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

The only way this fails is if the actual-source model includes a local corrected-package coordinate as an actual deformation:

\[
u(\tau)=u_0+\tau e_7+\cdots
\]

or

\[
u(\tau)=u_0+\tau e_Y+\cdots.
\]

That should be forbidden by definition of the actual-source manifold. If such package coordinates are allowed, D4 is being quotiented away by hand.

\[
\boxed{
\text{No free corrected-package coordinates in }T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
}

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\text{ contributes no column to }B_Y.
}
\]

# 3. \(E_2^Y\) local term

For \(E_2\), distinguish again:

- fixed completion;
- finite coherent amplitude;
- residual-controlled tail;
- local tangent freedom.

\boxed{
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

\Pi_YE_2=0

pointwise. A nonzero fixed \(E_2^Y\) basepoint or finite coherent \(E_2^Y\) amplitude can be routed to the amplitude layer. It only says:

}

c_E(\theta;p)=\frac{\Pi_YE_{\model}(\theta;p)}{y_Y(p)}.

D_\theta c_E=0

in the local fiber sense.

\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.

# 4. Labelled-realization local mismatch

For labelled realization, the local defect is controlled by the commutator

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0.
}
\]

A finite coherent commutator defect is acceptable at the amplitude layer. A local defect is not.

The likely proof splits as follows.

## \( \mathfrak C_Y^L \)

If source transition \(L\) is affine in the relevant slots,

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

so

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

If \(D^2L\neq0\), then

\[
DN_0[D^2L]
\]

has \(DN_0\)-origin, not \(D^2N_0\)-origin, and should be killed by the primitive \(Y12\) projection.

## \( \mathfrak C_Y^N \)

This is tensorial naturality:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

If \(N_0\) is defined functorially under the labelled transition, this vanishes.

If not, its mismatch is finite coherent only if it is a fixed expression depending on chart transition data, not atom-local freedom.

## \( \mathfrak C_Y^W \)

This is the same-point whitening transport defect. It vanishes if whitening is transported as a formed labelled carrier.

Moving-whitening artifacts such as

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E
\]

must remain separated from

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]]
\]

by the origin-pair label.

Thus the labelled-realization theorem needed for rank two is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0,
}
\]

with any finite coherent remainder routed to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).

---

# 5. Nuisance quotient

For rank two, nuisance must preserve the live pair:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

This is equivalent to saying \(e_7,e_Y\) survive as live coordinates with bounded duals.

Quantitatively:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

If nuisance contains a graph line

\[
(1,\rho_Y),
\]

rank two collapses to rank one. If it contains vertical

\[
(0,1),
\]

the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

This is not an arithmetic source relation unless the nuisance is justified by actual-source coherence. Otherwise it is quotient overcollapse.

---

# 6. Consolidated rank-two criterion

The rank-two branch is green if all of the following hold:

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

And D4 is rank two.

---

# 7. What if one condition fails?

The failure routing is now exact.

## Failure by graph line

If some local allowed direction gives

\[
(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then rank two collapses to rank one. The rank-one wall is

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

## Failure by vertical

If some local allowed direction gives

\[
(0,1),
\]

then the primitive \(Y12\) vertical direction is actual-source coherent. The \(Y12\) rank-one route dies locally.

## Failure by two independent directions

If local allowed directions span

\[
\mathbb C^2,
\]

the entire live fiber is actual-source/nuisance. The \(Y12\) route dies locally.

## Failure by finite coherent amplitude only

If a contaminant is finite coherent but not local, then \(B_Y=0\) may still hold, and the issue moves to vector amplitude Schur:

\[
F_{2Y}\notin \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

---

# 8. Rank-two amplitude object

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

define

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If a finite coherent amplitude space exists, define

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_{2Y}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
}
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}=0/\fixed,
\]

the wall is pointwise vector-valued.

---

# 9. Current best conclusion

Under the fixed-target EF-generated actual-source convention, the rank-two branch is now the best target:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0\text{ is plausible.}
}
\]

The strongest remaining local risks are:

\[
\boxed{
\text{free corrected-package coordinates accidentally included in }T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
E_2^Y\text{ has local, not merely finite coherent, carrier freedom;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ has local primitive fiber defect;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ overcollapses the live pair.}
}

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
}
\]

where

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

The strongest way to prove this is to show that every actual-source local direction is **EF-generated**, while the two live fiber directions

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y
\]

are **corrected-package carrier directions**. If that type separation is real, then neither \(s_7\) nor \(s_9^Y\) is actual-source local motion.

---

# 1. Define the type filtration

Introduce a filtration of local directions by origin type:

\[
\mathfrak F
=
\mathfrak F_{\EF}
\oplus
\mathfrak F_{\gamma}
\oplus
\mathfrak F_{\chart}
\oplus
\mathfrak F_{\tail}
\oplus
\mathfrak F_{\pkg}.
\]

Here:

\[
\mathfrak F_{\EF}
\]

contains EF-linear prime/dual atom variations;

\[
\mathfrak F_{\gamma}
\]

contains gamma/background terms;

\[
\mathfrak F_{\chart}
\]

contains coordinate, scale, and normalization effects;

\[
\mathfrak F_{\tail}
\]

contains nonfree completion/tail/residual terms;

\[
\mathfrak F_{\pkg}
\]

contains corrected-package live carrier directions such as

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y.
\]

The rank-two theorem needs:

\[
\boxed{
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathfrak F_{\EF}
+\mathfrak F_{\gamma}
+\mathfrak F_{\chart}
+\mathfrak F_{\tail}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
e_7,e_Y\in\mathfrak F_{\pkg}
}
\]

with

\[
\mathfrak F_{\pkg}
\cap
(\mathfrak F_{\EF}
+\mathfrak F_{\gamma}
+\mathfrak F_{\chart}
+\mathfrak F_{\tail})
=
0
\]

in the realized singular quotient, at polynomial angle.

This is the clean separation theorem.

---

# 2. The live covectors vanish on EF-generated directions

The live covectors

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

are dual to corrected-package carrier directions:

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\quad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0,\quad \ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

If the type separation holds, then

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(v)=\ell_9^Y(v)=0
}
\]

for every EF-generated direction

\[
v\in
\mathfrak F_{\EF}
+\mathfrak F_{\gamma}
+\mathfrak F_{\chart}
+\mathfrak F_{\tail}.
\]

So it is enough to prove that actual-source local directions are EF-generated and not package-generated.

---

# 3. Actual-source tangents are EF-generated

The fixed-target actual-source tangent model contains variations such as

\[
\partial_m q''_{\can},\quad
\partial_\lambda q''_{\can},\quad
\partial_{\phase}q''_{\can},\quad
\partial_{\chart}q''_{\can},\quad
\partial_{\gamma/\bg}q''_{\can}.
\]

Each has schematic form

\[
\sum_n
P_{\theta,n}(x,\tau_n)
a_n e^{i\tau_nx}
+
\text{dual/gamma/chart terms}.
\]

These are EF-linear or background/chart origin. They are not corrected-package carrier coordinates.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}=0.
}
\]

This proves the first large part of \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 4. Actual second curvature is still EF-generated

Second parameter derivatives have schematic form

\[
\partial_i\partial_j(a_ne^{i\tau_nx})
=
P_{ij}(x,\tau_n,\partial\tau_n,\partial a_n,\partial^2\tau_n,\partial^2a_n)e^{i\tau_nx}.
\]

They are second derivatives in parameter space, but they are not corrected-package carrier directions.

So, under fixed-\(\alpha\),

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

This is especially important: parameter curvature is not the same thing as a primitive \(Y12\) or live \(C_7\) carrier. Unless actual parameters change \(N_0\), \(DW_{\same}\), or the package coordinates themselves, second curvature remains outside the live fiber.

---

# 5. Lower actual directions are EF-generated unless package coordinates are admitted

The only way

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\]

can hit the live fiber is if the actual-source model contains a corrected-package coordinate.

If lower actual directions are EF/gamma/tail/chart-generated, then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

The formal carrier

\[
e_7
\]

does not count. It enters \(B_Y\) only if there is an actual family

\[
u(t)
\]

such that

\[
\left.\frac{d}{dt}\right|_{t=0}u(t)
\]

has nonzero \(\ell_7\)-coordinate.

Therefore the precise no-go guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\text{ contains no free local corrected-package carrier coordinates.}
}
\]

If this guardrail is accepted, then:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

This keeps rank two alive.

---

# 6. \(E_2^Y\) is local only if its coefficient map has local derivatives

For \(E_2\), define a primitive \(Y12\)-coefficient map

\[
c_E(\theta;p)
=
\frac{\Pi_YE_{\model}(\theta;p)}{y_Y(p)}
\]

where this expression makes sense.

A fixed \(E_2^Y\) contribution has

\[
D_\theta c_E=0.
\]

A residual-controlled contribution does not enter \(B_Y\).

A finite coherent contribution has a finite-dimensional amplitude space, but not local fiber freedom.

The local-rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_\theta c_E=0
\quad
\text{for all actual local parameters }\theta.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

If \(D_\theta c_E\neq0\) but the image is finite coherent, move it to the vector amplitude model

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

If it is atom-local, rank two fails at the amplitude/local interface.

---

# 7. Labelled-realization mismatch is local only if the commutator is local

The labelled/raw mismatch is controlled by

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

Rank two requires

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0.
}
\]

A finite coherent commutator is not a \(B_Y\) column; it belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

A residual commutator belongs to

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

The only local rank-two blocker is a commutator defect that creates local \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\) freedom.

Thus the labelled-realization theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y
=
0
+
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\lab}
+
R_{\lab,Y},
}
\]

with no locally free fiber term.

---

# 8. Nuisance must be type-homogeneous

For rank two, the nuisance quotient must not intersect the live package fiber:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If nuisance identifies package directions with EF-generated directions, then the type separation fails. That is quotient overcollapse.

So the nuisance theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ is homogeneous with respect to the EF/package type split near }e_7,e_Y.
}
\]

---

# 9. The master theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{EF-Generated Separation Implies \(B_Y=0\).}
}
\]

Assume:

1. the actual-source tangent model is generated by EF/gamma/tail/chart/normalization data, with no free local corrected-package carrier coordinates;

2. fixed-\(\alpha\) second curvature is parameter curvature, not package-carrier curvature;

3. \(E_2^Y\) has no local coefficient derivative in the live package fiber;

4. labelled-realization mismatch has no local package-fiber commutator, only zero/fixed/finite coherent/residual pieces;

5. the singular nuisance quotient is type-homogeneous and preserves the live pair.

Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Consequently D4 is rank two and produces the vector wall

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

---

# 10. What remains to verify

This master theorem reduces rank-two proof to four precise checks:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \text{No free package coordinates in }T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ D_\theta c_E=0\text{ locally in the live fiber.}}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ \mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0.}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \mathcal N_{\sing}\cap\operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}=0
\text{ with polynomial angle.}}
\]

If these hold, rank-two D4 should be the preferred downstream path.

## Agent 1 — TwoGradeLiveQuotient and live dual pair

### Nomenclature

I will use the existing convention:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\fixed}.
\]

\[
\rho_\Gamma
\]

is the rank-one wall ratio,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

\[
R_{\fold}(x)
\]

is the O2/O3 fold ratio,

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

This prompt focuses on the rank-two branch, where ideally \(B_Y=0\), so \(\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma\) never enter the main wall. The attached instruction identifies \(B_Y=0\) and JointFlatnessStability as the current rank-two centerpieces. 

---

# 1. Define the two-grade carrier space

Let

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}
\]

be the finite realized singular carrier space generated by:

1. order-seven carrier candidates, including

\[
e_7=\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_7];
\]

2. order-nine primitive \(Y12\)-carrier candidates, including

\[
e_Y=\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_{Y12}];
\]

3. all order-seven and order-nine terms produced by actual EF/gamma/chart/tail source data, actual parameter curvature, nuisance, and labelled transition realization.

The point is to put both live D4 directions into one common quotient, not separate \(C_7\) and \(Y12\) quotient systems.

---

# 2. Define the dead/coherent subspace \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

Let

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}\subset \mathcal J_{7,9}
\]

be the subspace generated by all nonlive actual/coherent directions:

\[
\mathcal J_{7}^{\EF\text{-lin}},
\quad
\mathcal J_{7}^{\EF\text{-curv}},
\quad
\mathcal J_{7}^{\chart},
\quad
\mathcal J_{7}^{\chart\text{-curv}},
\quad
\mathcal J_{7}^{\gamma/\bg},
\quad
\mathcal J_{7}^{\norm},
\]

and the analogous order-nine nonprimitive directions:

\[
\mathcal J_{9}^{\EF\text{-lin}},
\quad
\mathcal J_{9}^{\EF\text{-curv}},
\quad
\mathcal J_{9}^{\chart},
\quad
\mathcal J_{9}^{\gamma/\bg},
\quad
\mathcal J_{9}^{\norm},
\quad
\mathcal J_{9}^{\tail/\res}.
\]

Also include:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

and all wrong-origin order-nine terms, such as:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E_2,
\quad
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\quad
DN_0[D^2L],
\]

unless they have the exact primitive \(Y12\) signature.

The primitive \(Y12\) signature excluded from \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) is

\[
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

The live \(C_7\) carrier class is also excluded from \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\).

---

# 3. Two-grade live quotient

Define

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal Q_{2Y}:=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

The rank-two route needs:

\[
\boxed{
[e_7]\neq0,\qquad [e_Y]\neq0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
[e_7],[e_Y]\text{ linearly independent in }\mathcal Q_{2Y}.
}
\]

Quantitatively, choose a Riesz-normalized norm on \(\mathcal J_{7,9}\). Let

\[
P_{\mathcal D^\perp}
\]

be projection away from \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\). Set

\[
\widetilde e_7=P_{\mathcal D^\perp}e_7,
\qquad
\widetilde e_Y=P_{\mathcal D^\perp}e_Y.
\]

Then

\[
G_{\live}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\langle \widetilde e_7,\widetilde e_7\rangle&
\langle \widetilde e_7,\widetilde e_Y\rangle\\
\langle \widetilde e_Y,\widetilde e_7\rangle&
\langle \widetilde e_Y,\widetilde e_Y\rangle
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The finite condition is

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

This is the clean two-grade live-pair condition.

---

# 4. Construct the live dual covectors

If the Gram condition holds, define

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7\\
\ell_9^Y
\end{pmatrix}
=
G_{\live}^{-1}
\begin{pmatrix}
\langle \widetilde e_7,\cdot\rangle\\
\langle \widetilde e_Y,\cdot\rangle
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal D_{7,9})=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal D_{7,9})=0.
\]

The norms satisfy

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

Thus the primitive live fiber

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y)
\]

is defined by

\[
s_7(v)=\ell_7(v),
\qquad
s_9^Y(v)=\ell_9^Y(v).
\]

---

# 5. Why \(\ell_7\) is not raw \([z^7]\)

A key point:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7\neq [z^7].
}
\]

Raw order-seven source drift from translation, scaling, chart change, gamma/background variation, or EF parameter curvature lies in

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Therefore it is killed:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal J_{7}^{\EF\text{-lin}})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal J_{7}^{\chart})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal J_{7}^{\gamma/\bg})=0.
\]

So actual parameters may have raw seventh-order Taylor coefficients without moving \(s_7\). Only the live corrected-package \(C_7\) carrier survives in \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\).

This is the central guardrail for rank two.

---

# 6. Why \(\ell_9^Y\) is not generic order-nine detection

Similarly,

\[
\ell_9^Y
\]

does not read generic order-nine or generic degree-two terms. It reads the primitive \(Y12\) carrier signature:

\[
\boxed{
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
}
\]

Thus it kills:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E_2,
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\]

\[
DN_0[D^2L],
\]

\[
\partial_\theta^2(\mathrm{EF\ atom}),
\]

unless such terms are genuinely identified with the primitive \(Y12\) carrier in the singular quotient. If they are identified, that is quotient overcollapse and must be diagnosed.

---

# 7. Consequence for \(B_Y\)

With \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) constructed, define

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

But all components belonging to \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) are killed.

Therefore

\[
B_Y=0
\]

will follow if all actual/nuisance/local generators lie in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), except for possible finite coherent amplitude/residual pieces that do not enter local \(B_Y\).

This cleanly separates the local rank-two question from finite coherent amplitude issues.

---

# 8. Failure modes

The two-grade quotient fails in exactly these ways:

## Failure 1 — \(e_7\in\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

Then the live \(C_7\) carrier is actually EF/chart/gamma/nuisance. Rank two loses its first coordinate.

## Failure 2 — \(e_Y\in\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

Then primitive \(Y12\) is nuisance/coherent/wrongly quotiented. The \(Y12\) route dies locally.

## Failure 3 — \(e_Y\in\mathcal D_{7,9}+\mathbf C e_7\)

Then the two live coordinates collapse to one. Rank two fails.

## Failure 4 — quotient forgets origin labels

If the quotient identifies

\[
D^2N_0\text{-origin }Y12
\]

with EF-linear, chart, tail, or whitening-artifact terms, then \(\ell_9^Y\) is not well-defined as desired.

## Failure 5 — ill-conditioning

If

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ll Q^{-A},
\]

the duals exist algebraically but are analytically unusable.

---

# 9. Theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TwoGradeLiveQuotient.}
}
\]

There is a finite carrier quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

such that:

1. raw EF-linear, chart, gamma/background, normalization, tail, and parameter-curvature order-seven/order-nine directions vanish in \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\);

2. the live corrected-package carriers survive:

\[
[e_7],[e_Y]\neq0;
\]

3. they are polynomially transverse:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A};
\]

4. the resulting bounded duals

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

define the fiber coordinates

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

This is the first required theorem for the rank-two branch.

---

# 10. Current status

If TwoGradeLiveQuotient holds, then the next theorem

\[
\mathrm{CanonicalImageAvoidsLiveFiber}
\]

becomes much easier: it just says the actual-source image lies in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\).

That would imply

\[
B_Y=0
\]

unless \(E_2^Y\), labelled-realization mismatch, or nuisance introduces local live-package coordinates.

So the rank-two local proof reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
\text{construct }\mathcal Q_{2Y}
\quad\text{and prove actual-source generators lie in }\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

## Agent 1 — stress test of the two-grade dead space \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

I keep the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The proposed rank-two local quotient is

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}:=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

with live pair

\[
e_7=\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_7],
\]

\[
e_Y=\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_{Y12}].
\]

The quotient must satisfy two opposite requirements:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is large enough to kill actual EF-generated directions;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is small enough not to kill }e_7,e_Y.
}
\]

This is the exact rank-two live-pair balance.

---

# 1. \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) should be type-generated, not coefficient-generated

The dead space should not be defined by arbitrary coefficient cancellation. It should be generated by **types**:

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}
=
\mathcal D_{\EF}
+
\mathcal D_{\gamma}
+
\mathcal D_{\chart}
+
\mathcal D_{\norm}
+
\mathcal D_{\tail}
+
\mathcal D_{\nuis}
+
\mathcal D_{\wrong}.
\]

Where:

\[
\mathcal D_{\EF}
=
\text{EF-linear and EF-parameter-curvature directions},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\gamma}
=
\text{gamma/background directions},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\chart}
=
\text{coordinate/chart/transport directions},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\norm}
=
\text{gauge/normalization directions},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\tail}
=
\text{tail/residual/completion directions not primitive live-carrier},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\nuis}
=
\text{true singular nuisance},
\]

\[
\mathcal D_{\wrong}
=
\text{wrong-origin/wrong-branch/wrong-whitening/wrong-parity carrier types}.
\]

This type-generation prevents a dangerous move: choosing \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) after seeing \(e_7,e_Y\) so that it artificially kills or spares them.

---

# 2. Order-seven dead space must not contain \(C_7\)

At order seven, define the dead order-seven space:

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\norm}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

The live order-seven condition is:

\[
\boxed{
e_7\notin \mathcal D_7,
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(e_7,\mathcal D_7)\ge Q^{-A}\|e_7\|.
}
\]

This is exactly the \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness theorem.

It says:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ is not EF-linear drift, chart drift, gamma/background drift, or nuisance.}
}
\]

This is a finite Schur test.

If it fails, the rank-two route loses the first coordinate. Then either:

- rank-one \(Y12\)-only route remains possible, or
- the quotient is too coarse.

---

# 3. Order-nine dead space must not contain \(Y12\)

At order nine, the primitive \(Y12\) live condition is:

\[
\boxed{
e_Y\notin \mathcal D_9+\mathbf C e_7,
}
\]

more quantitatively:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_Y,\mathcal D_{7,9}+\mathbf C e_7)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
}
\]

Here \(\mathcal D_9\) includes:

\[
\EF\text{-linear},
\quad
\EF\text{-curvature},
\quad
\gamma/\bg,
\quad
\chart,
\quad
\norm,
\quad
\tail,
\quad
\text{wrong-origin degree-two terms}.
\]

The primitive \(Y12\) class has signature:

\[
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

Thus the condition says:

\[
\boxed{
Y12\text{ is not a tail/whitening artifact, chart curvature, EF curvature, or nuisance.}
}
\]

This is the order-nine faithfulness theorem.

---

# 4. Avoiding cross-grade contamination

Because \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\) combines order-seven and order-nine, we must also ensure that the quotient does not identify \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\) through a mixed relation.

The bad relation would be:

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

But \(e_7\) and \(e_Y\) have different grade/order and different origin labels. A type-homogeneous \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) should forbid such a relation.

So require:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is homogeneous in the two-grade decomposition.}
}
\]

Meaning, if

\[
v_7+v_9\in\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

with

\[
v_7\in\mathcal J_7,
\qquad
v_9\in\mathcal J_9,
\]

then

\[
v_7\in\mathcal D_7,
\qquad
v_9\in\mathcal D_9.
\]

This prevents order-seven/order-nine overcollapse.

---

# 5. Actual-source image should land in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

Once \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) is defined by type, the actual-source image condition is:

\[
\boxed{
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

This is easier to prove than direct coordinate vanishings.

For fixed-target actual source:

- \(\partial_m,\partial_\lambda\): EF-linear/chart type;
- phase/normalization: normalization/chart type;
- gamma/background: gamma/background type;
- second parameter curvature: EF-curvature/chart-curvature type;
- fixed completion: fixed basepoint or tail/residual type;
- residual completion: \(R_X^Y\), not local \(B_Y\).

Therefore all are in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), provided no actual-source parameter is a free package coordinate.

This proves:

\[
\ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=
\ell_9^Y(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

---

# 6. What must be excluded explicitly

The theorem must explicitly exclude:

\[
\boxed{
\text{free corrected-package coordinates in }T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual source parameter changing }N_0\text{ or }DW_{\same}\text{ as a package object;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{atom-local }E_2^Y\text{ coefficient freedom;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{labelled transition recomputing }N_0\text{ and creating local }e_Y;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{nuisance quotient identifying live package directions with dead EF directions.}
}
\]

These are the only meaningful ways \(B_Y=0\) can fail under the type-split framework.

---

# 7. Finite theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TwoGradeTypeFaithfulness.}
}
\]

There exists a finite two-grade quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

such that:

1. \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) contains all EF-linear, EF-curvature, chart, chart-curvature, gamma/background, normalization, tail/residual, and true nuisance directions;

2. \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) is homogeneous in the order-seven/order-nine decomposition and in the refined origin labels;

3. \(e_7,e_Y\) survive with polynomial separation:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A};
\]

4. all actual-source local tangent directions lie in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\);

5. local \(E_2^Y\) and labelled commutator contributions are either in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), finite coherent amplitude, or residual-controlled, but not local live-fiber directions.

Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

This theorem is exactly the local rank-two gateway.

---

# 8. Consequence for D4

Under TwoGradeTypeFaithfulness,

\[
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}=M_{\D4}.
\]

Since

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad c_7\alpha_9\ne0,
\]

D4 is rank two.

The wall is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Coefficient conditioning gives:

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
}
\]

This reduces the downstream wall to joint flatness rather than moving-line finite type.

---

# 9. Remaining proof work

The theorem reduces all local algebra to four finite audits:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ [e_7]\neq0\text{ in }\mathcal Q_{2Y}.}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ [e_Y]\neq0\text{ and independent of }[e_7]\text{ in }\mathcal Q_{2Y}.}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\subset\mathcal D_{7,9}.}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ E_2^Y,\mathfrak C_Y\text{ have no local live-fiber components.}}
\]

If these pass, the rank-two D4 branch is locally closed.

## Agent 1 — constructing annihilators for \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\)

I keep the fixed notation:

\[

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TwoGradeTypeFaithfulness}
}
\]

with quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Now the task is to make this non-handwavy by constructing separating covectors for the two live classes.

---

# 1. Desired annihilators

We need two covectors

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

such that:

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal D_{7,9})=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal D_{7,9})=0.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\in \mathcal D_{7,9}^{\perp}
}
\]

and they are dual to

\[
[e_7],[e_Y]\in\mathcal Q_{2Y}.
\]

The practical way to build them is by signature projection, not by raw monomial coefficient extraction.

---

# 2. Candidate for \(\ell_7\)

The order-seven live covector should be a projection to the live \(C_7\)-carrier quotient:

\[
\ell_7=\Pi_{[C_7]}^{\vee}.
\]

It must annihilate the order-seven dead space:

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\norm}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

So the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
[C_7]\notin\mathcal D_7.
}
\]

Quantitatively:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_7,\mathcal D_7)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_7\|.
}
\]

This is the \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness theorem.

If this holds, the normalized annihilator is

\[
\ell_7
=
\frac{(I-P_{\mathcal D_7})e_7}
{\|(I-P_{\mathcal D_7})e_7\|^2}
\]

up to the later orthogonalization against \(e_Y\). Since \(e_Y\) is order nine and type-distinct, the cross correction should be zero or polynomially small.

---

# 3. Candidate for \(\ell_9^Y\)

The order-nine \(Y12\) covector should project to the primitive origin sector:

\[
\Sigma_Y
=
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

So define

\[
\ell_9^Y=\Pi_{\Sigma_Y}^{\vee}.
\]

It must annihilate:

\[
\mathcal J_9^{\EF\text{-lin}},
\quad
\mathcal J_9^{\EF\text{-curv}},
\quad
\mathcal J_9^{\chart},
\quad
\mathcal J_9^{\gamma/\bg},
\quad
\mathcal J_9^{\norm},
\quad
\mathcal J_9^{\tail/\res},
\]

and all wrong-origin terms such as

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E_2,
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\]

\[
DN_0[D^2L],
\]

\[
\partial_\theta^2(\text{EF atom}).
\]

The finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
(e_Y,\mathcal D_9+\mathbf C e_7)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
}
\]

This is the primitive \(Y12\)-origin faithfulness theorem.

---

# 4. Homogeneity requirement

The two-grade quotient must not allow mixed order-seven/order-nine relations:

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

A sufficient condition is that

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

is homogeneous under the two-grade decomposition:

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}=\mathcal J_7\oplus \mathcal J_9.
\]

That is,

\[
v_7+v_9\in\mathcal D_{7,9}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
v_7\in\mathcal D_7,\quad v_9\in\mathcal D_9.
\]

This is natural if \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) is type-generated rather than coefficient-fitted.

Thus one required lemma is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TwoGradeHomogeneity.}
}
\]

Without it, the live pair can collapse even if each component looks live separately.

---

# 5. Why actual source lies in \(\ker(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\)

Once the covectors are type-defined, actual-source vanishings are immediate if actual-source directions are EF-generated.

For any

\[
U\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

generated by EF/gamma/chart/normalization/tail data,

\[
U\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Therefore

\[
\ell_7(U)=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(U)=0.
\]

This proves

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
\]

So the proof of \(B_Y=0\) is reduced to:

\[
\boxed{
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

That is exactly the EF-generated actual-source separation principle.

---

# 6. The remaining subtle term: canonical completion

The completion \(E_2\) must be placed carefully.

If \(E_2\) is fixed or residual-controlled, it is not a local tangent and does not enter \(B_Y\).

If it produces finite coherent amplitudes, it enters

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

not \(B_Y\).

If it produces local derivatives in the live fiber, rank two fails.

Thus in the quotient proof:

\[
E_2^{\local}\in \mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

or zero, while

\[
E_2^{\coh}
\]

is not part of the local quotient and is handled by vector amplitude Schur.

The finite local test is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2=0.
}
\]

---

# 7. The labelled commutator term

Similarly, the labelled/raw mismatch

\[
R_{\lab}
\]

does not need to vanish globally. It must satisfy:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
\]

Any finite coherent mismatch goes into

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

and any residual mismatch goes into

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

The local commutator test is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0.
}
\]

A sufficient algebraic condition is that the commutator terms are wrong-origin relative to both \(C_7\) and primitive \(Y12\):

\[
DN_0[D^2L]\in \mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E\in \mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

unless they exactly equal the formed live carriers. If they exactly equal live carriers with local coefficients, rank two fails.

---

# 8. Master proof plan

To prove \(B_Y=0\), establish the following chain:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_7,\mathcal D_7)\ge Q^{-A}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_Y,\mathcal D_9+\mathbf C e_7)\ge Q^{-A}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is two-grade homogeneous}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}=0
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0
}
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 9. If this fails

Failure routing:

## \(e_7\) fails

If

\[
e_7\in\mathcal D_7,
\]

the first component of rank-two D4 dies. The route may reduce to a \(Y12\)-only scalar wall.

## \(e_Y\) fails

If

\[
e_Y\in\mathcal D_9+\mathbf C e_7,
\]

the primitive \(Y12\) obstruction dies or collapses.

## actual source not in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

If actual-source directions hit \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\), rank two fails locally. If only a graph line appears, rank one remains.

## \(E_2\) or labelled mismatch local

If they produce local live-fiber derivatives, rank two fails. If finite coherent, amplitude Schur remains.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The rank-two local theorem is now a typed quotient exactness statement:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal Q_{2Y}
\text{ separates live package carriers from EF-generated actual source.}
}
\]

If this quotient exists with polynomial conditioning, then \(B_Y=0\) follows unless \(E_2\) or labelled realization introduces local live-fiber freedom.

This is the cleanest local target for the rank-two route.

## Agent 1 — TwoGradeLiveQuotient

No nomenclature changes.

\]

so that D4 remains rank two with

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H

c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp_{Q^A}
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

The attached coordinator prompt correctly centers this round on proving or breaking \(B_Y=0\), with TwoGradeLiveQuotient as the local algebraic starting point. 

---

# 1. Define the two-grade carrier space \(\mathcal J_{7,9}\)

Let

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}
=
\mathcal J_7\oplus\mathcal J_9
\]

be the finite realized carrier space containing:

\[
\mathcal J_7:
\text{ all order-seven singular/transition carrier candidates};
\]

\[
\mathcal J_9:
\text{ all order-nine singular/transition carrier candidates relevant to primitive }Y12.
\]

The two live vectors are:

\[
e_7
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_7],
\]

\[
e_Y
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}].
\]

Here \(e_7\) is the live \(C_7\)-carrier, not raw order-seven Taylor mass. \(e_Y\) is the primitive \(Y12\)-carrier, not generic order-nine degree-two mass.

---

# 2. Define the dead denominator \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\)

The denominator must kill actual EF-generated motion, chart/gauge motion, gamma/background motion, tails, residuals, and nuisance, but must **not** kill \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\).

Define

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}
=
\mathcal D_7\oplus \mathcal D_9
\]

with \(\mathcal D_7\subset\mathcal J_7\) and \(\mathcal D_9\subset\mathcal J_9\).

At order seven:

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\norm}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\norm\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

At order nine:

\[
\mathcal D_9
=
\mathcal J_9^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\norm}
+
\mathcal J_9^{\tail/\res}
+
\mathcal N_9
+
\mathcal J_9^{\wrong}.
\]

Here \(\mathcal J_9^{\wrong}\) includes all wrong-origin or superficially similar degree-two terms, such as

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E_2,
\qquad
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\qquad
DN_0[D^2L],
\qquad
\partial_\theta^2(\mathrm{EF\ atom}),
\]

unless a term genuinely has the primitive \(Y12\) signature

\[
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

This definition is type-generated, not coefficient-fitted. That is essential.

---

# 3. Define the quotient

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal Q_{2Y}:=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

The desired live pair condition is:

\[
[e_7]\neq0,\qquad [e_Y]\neq0,
\]

and

\[
[e_7],[e_Y]\text{ linearly independent in }\mathcal Q_{2Y}.
\]

Quantitatively, choose a Riesz-normalized norm and define

\[
\widetilde e_7=P_{\mathcal D_{7,9}^{\perp}}e_7,
\qquad
\widetilde e_Y=P_{\mathcal D_{7,9}^{\perp}}e_Y.
\]

Then

\[
G_{\live}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\langle\widetilde e_7,\widetilde e_7\rangle&
\langle\widetilde e_7,\widetilde e_Y\rangle\\
\langle\widetilde e_Y,\widetilde e_7\rangle&
\langle\widetilde e_Y,\widetilde e_Y\rangle
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The finite condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

This is the exact Schur/Gram condition for the live two-grade fiber.

---

# 4. Construct the live covectors

If the Gram condition holds, define

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7\\
\ell_9^Y
\end{pmatrix}
=
G_{\live}^{-1}
\begin{pmatrix}
\langle \widetilde e_7,\cdot\rangle\\
\langle \widetilde e_Y,\cdot\rangle
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal D_{7,9})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal D_{7,9})=0.
\]

These covectors define

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(v)
=
(\ell_7(v),\ell_9^Y(v)).
\]

This is the mathematically clean version of the local fiber \((s_7,s_9^Y)\).

---

# 5. Why \(\ell_7\) is not raw order-seven extraction

A key guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7\neq [z^7].
}
\]

Actual EF variations may have raw order-seven Taylor coefficients. They vanish in \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\) if they lie in \(\mathcal D_7\).

So the correct statement is:

\[
[z^7]\partial_\theta q''_{\can}\neq0
\quad\not\Rightarrow\quad
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})\neq0.
\]

The \(s_7\)-coordinate detects only the live \(C_7\)-carrier class.

Thus the order-seven rank-two audit becomes:

\[
A_7\subseteq \operatorname{ran}D_7,
\]

but

\[
\operatorname{dist}(c_7^{\live},\operatorname{ran}D_7)\ge Q^{-A}|c_7^{\live}|.
\]

This is the \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness problem.

---

# 6. Why \(\ell_9^Y\) is not generic order-nine extraction

Likewise,

\[
\ell_9^Y
\]

does not detect arbitrary order-nine, arbitrary degree-two, or arbitrary whitening terms. It detects the primitive \(Y12\) signature:

\[
\boxed{
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
}
\]

It should kill:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]E_2,
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\]

\[
DN_0[D^2L],
\]

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}(\mathrm{EF\ atom}),
\]

unless these are actually identified with primitive \(Y12\) by the singular quotient. If that happens, the quotient is over-collapsed or \(Y12\) is not a distinct live carrier.

---

# 7. Two-grade homogeneity

The denominator must be homogeneous in the two grades:

\[
v_7+v_9\in\mathcal D_{7,9},
\quad
v_7\in\mathcal J_7,\ v_9\in\mathcal J_9
\]

should imply

\[
v_7\in\mathcal D_7,
\qquad
v_9\in\mathcal D_9.
\]

This prevents an artificial relation

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Without two-grade homogeneity, \(e_7\) and \(e_Y\) may each look live separately but collapse in the combined quotient.

Thus TwoGradeLiveQuotient requires:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}=\mathcal D_7\oplus\mathcal D_9
}
\]

or at least polynomially controlled block-triangularity with no live-pair collision.

---

# 8. Relation to \(B_Y=0\)

Once \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\) and \((\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\) are built,

\[
B_Y=0
\]

is equivalent to:

\[
\ell_7(U)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(U)=0
\]

for every

\[
U\in
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}.
\]

But if these \(U\)'s lie in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), this follows automatically.

So the local rank-two proof becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual/local/nuisance objects lie in }\mathcal D_{7,9};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
e_7,e_Y\text{ survive outside }\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

That is the core exactness statement.

---

# 9. The TwoGradeLiveQuotient theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TwoGradeLiveQuotient.}
}
\]

There exists a finite quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}=\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

such that:

1. \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) contains EF-linear, EF-curvature, chart, chart-curvature, gamma/background, normalization, normalization-curvature, tail/residual, wrong-origin, and true nuisance directions;

2. \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) is homogeneous across the order-seven/order-nine decomposition;

3. the live carriers survive independently:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A};
\]

4. the dual covectors

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

are polynomially bounded and annihilate \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\).

This theorem supplies the live fiber for rank-two D4.

---

# 10. Failure modes

The theorem fails if:

\[
e_7\in\mathcal D_7,
\]

meaning \(C_7\) is actually dead EF/chart/gamma/nuisance drift;

\[
e_Y\in\mathcal D_9+\mathbf C e_7,
\]

meaning primitive \(Y12\) is dead or collapses into order-seven;

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is too small},
\]

so actual EF-generated objects leak into the live fiber;

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ is too large},
\]

so it kills live D4 carriers;

or

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ll Q^{-A}.
\]

---

# 11. Current conclusion

This quotient is the right local object.

If TwoGradeLiveQuotient is proved, then the remaining \(B_Y=0\) proof reduces to showing:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\subseteq
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

That is exactly the typed exactness theorem needed for the rank-two route.

\[
\theta\in
\{m,\lambda,\phase,\chart,\norm,\gamma/\bg\}.
\]

Locally, these have the schematic form

\[
\partial_\theta(a_ne^{i\tau_nx})
=
(\partial_\theta a_n+i a_n\partial_\theta\tau_n\,x)e^{i\tau_nx},
\]

plus dual/gamma/chart terms.

Thus they belong to

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\norm}.
\]

By definition these are contained in

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

This is the first key typed-exactness step.

Important: this does **not** require raw order-seven or raw order-nine Taylor coefficients to vanish. It only requires that those coefficients are EF/chart/gamma/normalization-type and hence dead in \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\).

---

# 2. Actual second curvatures

Now take

\[
U=\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}.
\]

For EF atoms,

\[
\partial_i\partial_j(a_ne^{i\tau_nx})
=
P_{ij}(x,\tau_n,\partial \tau_n,\partial a_n,\partial^2\tau_n,\partial^2a_n)e^{i\tau_nx}.
\]

This is second-order in parameter space, but still EF-origin. Therefore it belongs to

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\norm\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\gamma/\bg}.
\]

These are included in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\). So:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This is the clean fixed-\(\alpha\) result.

If \(\alpha\) is treated only as proof-thickening for O2/O3, it is not part of \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\). If \(\alpha\) is made actual-source internal, then \(\partial_\alpha,\partial_\alpha^2\) must be added to the above list and re-audited. Under ordinary weight/phase deformation they remain EF-origin; if \(\alpha\) changes \(N_0\), \(DW_{\same}\), or package coordinates, rank two may fail.

---

# 3. Actual lower directions

This is subtler.

Let

\[
U\in \mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}.
\]

The question is whether \(U\) is an actual-source deformation or only a formal D4/lower carrier.

If \(U\) is EF/gamma/tail/chart-generated, then

\[
U\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

If \(U\) is a formal package carrier

\[
e_7
\]

or formal continuation

\[
e_7\mapsto \rho_Ye_Y,
\]

that does **not** put it in \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\). Formal carriers are not actual-source motion unless the actual-source model explicitly admits them.

So the lower actual theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

Equivalent operationally:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

This is true if actual lower directions are generated by the canonical EF/gamma/tail/chart source and not by free corrected-package coordinates.

The only failure is a package-coordinate leak:

\[
U=e_7+\cdots
\quad\text{or}\quad
U=e_Y+\cdots
\]

inside the actual-source tangent model. That should be excluded by definition of actual-source arithmetic realization.

---

# 4. Local \(E_2^Y\) freedom

Now consider

\[
U\in\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}.
\]

A nonzero fixed \(E_2^Y\) basepoint is not a tangent. A finite coherent \(E_2^Y\) amplitude belongs to amplitude Schur, not local \(B_Y\). A residual-controlled \(E_2^Y\) piece belongs to \(R_X^Y\).

Thus \(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}\) consists only of extraction-local degrees of freedom in the completion.

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}\subseteq \mathcal D_{7,9}
}
\]

or simply

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

A useful coefficient-map test is:

\[
c_E(p)
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2(p)
=
(\ell_7(E_2(p)),\ell_9^Y(E_2(p))).
\]

Then local rank-two safety is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_E=0,
\qquad
D_{\loc}^2c_E=0.
}
\]

If \(c_E\) is fixed or finite coherent, it does not enter \(B_Y\). If \(D_{\loc}c_E\neq0\) in the live fiber, rank two collapses or dies depending on the image:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y) \Rightarrow \text{rank one},
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)\text{ or }\mathbb C^2 \Rightarrow \text{local death}.
\]

---

# 5. Local labelled-realization defect

Let

\[
U\in\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}.
\]

This is generated by the local part of

\[
R_{\lab}
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
-
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}\operatorname{Forget}_{\actual}.
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

The finite coherent part can enter

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

and the residual part can enter

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

The local part is controlled by the typed commutator

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

Rank-two safety requires:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
(\ell_7(R_{\lab}),\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}))
=0.
}
\]

Concretely:

- \(DN_0[D^2L]\) is \(DN_0\)-origin, hence dead unless the quotient forgets origin.
- \(D^2N_0\)-naturality defects should be zero or finite coherent.
- moving-whitening artifacts such as \(DW[\xi]E_2\) and \(D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0\) are wrong-origin relative to primitive \(Y12\) and should be dead.

So local labelled defects should lie in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), while finite coherent defects are routed to amplitude Schur.

---

# 6. Nuisance

Finally,

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

must be contained in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) but must not contain \(e_7,e_Y\).

The correct condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If nuisance touches the live fiber, that is quotient overcollapse unless justified by actual-source coherence.

---

# 7. Typed exactness theorem

Putting the pieces together:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TypedExactness}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

while \(e_7,e_Y\) survive independently in

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

This is the local rank-two gateway.

---

# 8. Consequence: rank-two D4

Under \(B_Y=0\),

\[
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}=M_{\D4},
\]

with

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Since

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

the D4 output

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\]

is polynomially equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

under coefficient conditioning.

This is the desired rank-two local obstruction.

---

# 9. Remaining exact blockers

The rank-two local theorem can fail only if:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual-source model includes free corrected-package coordinates;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
E_2^Y\text{ has extraction-local live-fiber derivatives;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
R_{\lab}\text{ has extraction-local live-fiber commutator defect;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ touches }e_7\text{ or }e_Y;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal D_{7,9}\text{ kills }e_7\text{ or }e_Y.
}
\]

These are now the precise local algebraic targets.

# 1. Turn typed exactness into a finite matrix test

Let

\[

be a finite spanning list for the local actual/nuisance/fiber-generating space

\[
\mathcal A_{\actual}.

After constructing the live covectors

\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y,

define the \(2\times M\) matrix

\[
\mathsf B_Y
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(U_1)&\ell_7(U_2)&\cdots&\ell_7(U_M)\\
\ell_9^Y(U_1)&\ell_9^Y(U_2)&\cdots&\ell_9^Y(U_M)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\mathsf B_Y=0.
}
\]

This is the exact finite audit.

The rank-one and death branches are also visible from the same matrix:

\[

\operatorname{ran}\mathsf B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)

gives the rank-one moving-wall branch.

\[

or

kills the current \(Y12\) local route.

\text{for every actual/local generator }U_j.

\quad

The typed exactness proof then becomes six finite assertions.

## 2.1 Actual first tangents

For

\[
U\in \mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual},
\]

we need

\[

\[
U\in
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\EF\text{-lin}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\norm}
\subseteq
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Thus

\[
\ell_7(U)=\ell_9^Y(U)=0.
\]

This kills raw seventh-order and ninth-order actual-source jet drift, provided it is dead-type drift.

---

## 2.2 Actual second curvature

For

\[
U\in\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\]

we need

\[
U\in
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\EF\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\chart\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\norm\text{-curv}}
+
\mathcal J_{7,9}^{\gamma/\bg}
\subseteq
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Equivalently:

\[

---

## 2.3 Actual lower directions

For

\[
U\in\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\]

the needed statement is:

U\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.

\[

}
\]

The formal carrier \(e_7\) and the formal connection

\[
e_7\mapsto \rho_Y e_Y
\]

are not automatically actual directions.

So the required finite assertion is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\cap \operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}=0
\quad\text{mod } \mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

If this fails through \(e_7\), rank two collapses to rank one.

If it fails through \(e_Y\), current \(Y12\) dies locally.

---

## 2.4 Local \(E_2^Y\) freedom

For

\[
U\in\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local},
\]

rank two needs

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}U=0.
\]

This is stronger than “finite coherent \(E_2^Y\) is harmless.” The local derivative must vanish:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2=0.
\]

Fixed \(E_2^Y\), finite coherent \(E_2^Y\), and residual-controlled \(E_2^Y\) do not enter \(B_Y\).

Only extraction-local coefficient freedom enters.

Thus the exact test is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2)\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2)
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

---

## 2.5 Local labelled-realization defect

For

\[
U\in\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local},
\]

rank two needs

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}U=0.
\]

Using the commutator split

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^W,
\]

the exact local test is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

Finite coherent mismatch is allowed and moves to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Residual mismatch is allowed and moves to

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

Local fiber mismatch is not allowed for rank two.

---

## 2.6 Nuisance

For nuisance,

\[
U\in\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

rank two needs

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
\]

This is equivalent to saying the live pair survives the quotient:

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

If nuisance touches \(e_7\), rank two collapses or dies. If nuisance touches \(e_Y\), the \(Y12\) route dies.

---

# 3. Strong form: typed exactness by inclusion

Instead of checking columns one at a time, prove the stronger inclusion:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal A_{\actual}\subseteq \mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal A_{\actual}=0
\]

because

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

annihilate

\[
\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

This is the cleanest version:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TypedExactness}_{2Y}:
\quad
\mathcal A_{\actual}\subseteq \mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

Together with TwoGradeLiveQuotient, this proves \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 4. What this buys

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

then D4 remains rank two:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

With

\[
|c_7|,|\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A},
\qquad
|\beta_9|\le Q^A,
\]

we get

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
}
\]

The rank-one moving wall disappears. No \(\rho_\Gamma\) honesty theorem is needed. No moving-line finite type. No forced ODE trap.

---

# 5. Remaining finite no-gos

The rank-two branch fails locally in these exact ways:

## No-go A — live pair does not survive

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})<Q^{-A}.
\]

## No-go B — actual source has package-coordinate motion

\[
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\not\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

because it contains \(e_7\), \(e_Y\), or their graph.

## No-go C — \(E_2^Y\) has local fiber derivatives

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2\neq0.
\]

## No-go D — labelled/raw transition has local fiber defect

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}\neq0.
\]

## No-go E — nuisance overcollapse

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}\neq0.
\]

If the failure is graph-like \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), rank one remains. If vertical or rank two, the current \(Y12\) branch dies locally.

---

# 6. Next exact proof step

The next proof should instantiate

\[
\mathcal A_{\actual}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

for each typed source:

\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9};
}
\]

\[

}

}
\]

If this holds, \(E_2^Y\) contributes nothing to \(B_Y\). If \(c_E\) is nonzero but fixed or finite coherent, rank two remains locally viable and the issue moves to vector amplitude Schur.

Failure routing:

- \(D_{\loc}c_E\in\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\): rank two collapses to rank one;
- \(D_{\loc}c_E\in\mathbf C(0,1)\): \(Y12\) vertical is actual/local, route dies;
- \(D_{\loc}c_E\) spans \(\mathbb C^2\): local live fiber is absorbed.

---

# 5. Local labelled-realization mismatch

For rank two, the local labelled/raw transition defect must vanish in the live fiber:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

The commutator split is

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

The expected routing is:

- \(DN_0[D^2L]\) is \(DN_0\)-origin and lies in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\);
- tensorial \(D^2N_0\) naturality mismatch is zero or finite coherent;
- moving-whitening artifacts are wrong-origin unless they exactly equal the formed primitive carrier.

Rank two requires there be no **local live-fiber** defect. Finite coherent mismatch is allowed at the amplitude layer; residual mismatch is allowed at the completion layer.

So the local condition is not

\[
R_{\lab}=0.
\]

It is:

\[
\boxed{
R_{\lab}^{\local}\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
}
\]

---

# 6. Nuisance

The nuisance subspace must be included in \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\), but it must not contain the live pair:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
e_7,e_Y\notin\mathcal N_{\sing}
}
\]

with polynomial separation.

Quantitatively, this is included in

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

If nuisance touches the live fiber, rank two is killed by quotient design, not by arithmetic source coherence. That should be treated as nuisance overcollapse.

---

# 7. TypedExactness theorem, termwise version

The theorem now reads:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TypedExactness}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
\quad\text{or is zero},
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
\quad\text{or is zero},
\]

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9},
\]

while \(e_7,e_Y\) survive independently in

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 8. Consequence

With

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

D4 rank is

\[
\operatorname{rank}M_{\D4}=2
\]

because

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

The D4 output is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix},
\]

and coefficient conditioning gives

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

This is the rank-two vector wall.

---

# 9. The only remaining local risks

The termwise analysis leaves exactly four local risks:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\not\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
}
\]

because actual source secretly contains package-carrier motion;

\[
\boxed{
2.\ D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ overcollapses }e_7,e_Y.
}
\]

If these four are excluded, the local rank-two theorem is essentially closed.

# 1. What \(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\) is allowed to contain

The actual lower-source space should include only lower data generated by the canonical arithmetic source:

\[
\text{EF prime/dual atoms},
\quad
\gamma/\text{background},
\quad
\text{chart/normalization},
\quad
\text{canonical tail/completion}.
\]

It should **not** include formal local package variables such as

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y,
\]

unless those variables are produced by an actual arithmetic deformation.

So the correct definition is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\EF}
+
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\gamma/\bg}
+
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\chart}
+
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\tail}
+
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\norm}.
}
\]

Under this definition,

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{7,9}
\]

by construction, provided \(\mathcal D_{7,9}\) includes those types.

The dangerous alternative is:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\EF/\gamma/\chart/\tail}
+
\mathbf C e_7
+
\mathbf C e_Y
+\cdots.
\]

That would add formal package coordinates to the actual-source tangent model and destroy the obstruction by definition. This must be rejected unless there is an arithmetic source family realizing those coordinates.

---

# 2. Criterion for actual \(C_7\)-motion

The actual \(C_7\)-motion question is:

\[
\exists u(\tau)\in\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\quad\text{such that}\quad
\ell_7\left(\left.\frac{d}{d\tau}\right|_{\tau=0}u(\tau)\right)\neq0?
\]

The answer should be **no** for the fixed canonical source model.

Why? Because an admissible \(u(\tau)\) must come from EF/gamma/chart/tail data. Such a derivative lies in the dead denominator:

\[
\left.\frac{d}{d\tau}\right|_{\tau=0}u(\tau)\in\mathcal D_{7,9}.
\]

Since

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal D_{7,9})=0,
\]

we get

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7\left(\left.\frac{d}{d\tau}\right|_{\tau=0}u(\tau)\right)=0.
}
\]

Thus \(C_7\) is D4-carrier motion, not actual-source motion.

This gives the desired rank-two conclusion, at least for the \(s_7\)-coordinate.

---

# 3. Criterion for actual primitive \(Y12\)-motion

Similarly, the actual primitive \(Y12\)-motion question is:

\[
\exists u(\tau)\in\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\quad\text{such that}\quad
\ell_9^Y\left(\left.\frac{d}{d\tau}\right|_{\tau=0}u(\tau)\right)\neq0?
\]

Again, the expected answer is **no**.

The primitive \(Y12\) signature is

\[
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [C_{Y12}]).
\]

Actual lower EF/gamma/chart/tail data has type

\[
\EF\text{-linear},
\quad
\gamma/\bg,
\quad
\chart,
\quad
\tail,
\quad
\norm.
\]

Even when differentiated or transported, it does not become the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\) carrier unless the source model itself introduces nonlinear package coordinates.

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

Together with the \(s_7\)-row,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

---

# 4. Formal lower connection is not an actual tangent

The formal lower connection

\[
e_7\mapsto \rho_Y e_Y
\]

does not contradict the above.

It says:

\[
\boxed{
\text{if actual-source data moves in }e_7,\text{ its formal }Y12\text{ continuation is }\rho_Ye_Y.
}
\]

But if

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

the antecedent never occurs in the actual-source tangent model. Hence the graph line

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

does **not** enter \(B_Y\).

So the formal connection is a conditional relation inside the package algebra, not an automatic quotient direction.

---

# 5. Finite audit: lower package-coordinate leak

The lower actual-source inclusion can now be reduced to a single finite test.

Let

\[
\mathcal P_{\pkg}:=\operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}
\]

inside \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\).

Then test:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{proj}_{\mathcal P_{\pkg}}
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

A concrete matrix version:

choose lower actual generators

\[
u_1,\ldots,u_M\in\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}.
\]

Compute

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(u_1)&\cdots&\ell_7(u_M)\\
\ell_9^Y(u_1)&\cdots&\ell_9^Y(u_M)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Rank-two needs this matrix to be zero.

If nonzero, classify:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\Rightarrow
\text{rank two collapses to rank one};
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)
\Rightarrow
\text{vertical local freedom; }Y12\text{ dies locally};
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2
\Rightarrow
\text{full local absorption; }Y12\text{ dies locally}.
\]

---

# 6. Relation to the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient

At order seven, the precise version is:

\[
A_7^{\lower}
\subseteq \operatorname{ran}D_7,
\]

where

\[
A_7^{\lower}
=
[\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}]_7
\]

is the raw order-seven lower actual-source image, and

\[
D_7
\]

is the dead denominator containing EF-linear, chart, gamma/background, normalization, curvature, and nuisance order-seven directions.

The live carrier satisfies

\[
\operatorname{dist}(c_7^{\live},\operatorname{ran}D_7)\ge Q^{-A}|c_7^{\live}|.
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{lower actual raw order-seven drift may exist, but it dies in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
}
\]

This is the finite, quotient-level statement that \(C_7\) is not actual-source motion.

---

# 7. Relation to primitive \(Y12\)-origin quotient

At order nine, the precise version is:

\[
[\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}]_9
\subseteq \mathcal D_9.
\]

That is, lower actual directions do not contain the primitive origin tuple

\[
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\,12,\ \same,\ w=1,\ [C_{Y12}]).
\]

If a lower actual direction has order-nine mass, it is EF/chart/gamma/tail-origin or wrong-origin degree-two. Hence it is dead in \(\mathcal Q_{2Y}\).

So lower actual directions do not move \(s_9^Y\).

---

# 8. The theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualLowerLiveFiberMotion.}
}
\]

\[

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}

\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

This removes the main lower-source blocker for \(B_Y=0\).

---

}
\]

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

If these also hold, then

\[

\]

and rank-two D4 is the correct downstream branch.

which is polynomially equivalent to \(H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\) under coefficient conditioning. The prompt identifies nuisance overcollapse as one of the remaining local risks.

# 1. What \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) is allowed to kill

The singular nuisance space should contain only genuine redundancies:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_{\gauge}
+
\mathcal N_{\coord}
+
\mathcal N_{\rep}
+
\mathcal N_{\tail}
+
\mathcal N_{\normalization}
+
\mathcal N_{\wrong\text{-}origin},

where these are:

- representative or gauge redundancies;
- coordinate/trivialization artifacts;
- normalization conventions;
- tail/residual denominators;
- source-linear chart artifacts;
- wrong-origin order-nine artifacts such as \(DN_0[D^2L]\), \(D\mathcal W[\xi]E\), or \(D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0\).

It must **not** contain the live corrected-package carrier directions

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y,
\]

nor a relation between them.

So the nuisance principle is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ kills representation artifacts, not live package carriers.}
}

# 2. Required finite condition

Inside the two-grade quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}
=
\mathcal J_{7,9}/\mathcal D_{7,9},

the live pair must survive with polynomial angle.

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_7,\mathcal D_{7,9})\ge Q^{-A}\|e_7\|;
}

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(e_Y,\mathcal D_{7,9}+\mathbf C e_7)\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
}
