## Agent 1 — reducing \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) to two finite Jacobians

I will keep the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The rank-two local target is still:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

The current remaining local obstruction is not whether \(E_2\) or \(R_{\lab}\) is nonzero. It is whether either has **atom-local live-fiber derivative**. The latest architecture states the finite certificate as \((I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0\) plus a live-pair Schur lower, so local \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) columns belong in \(A_{2Y}\) only if their local Jacobians are nonzero. 

---

# 1. The two local Jacobians

For rank two, define the live-fiber projection

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}X
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(X)\\
\ell_9^Y(X)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The two relevant Jacobians are:

\[
J_{E,p}^{\loc}
:=
D_{\eta_p}^{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2(p))
\end{pmatrix},
\]

and

\[
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
:=
D_{\eta_p}^{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Here \(\eta_p\) denotes atom-local variables: local completion counterterms, local chart refits, local whitening refits, local normal-form refits, local representative choices, or local nuisance representatives chosen with access to the live-fiber residual.

The rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
J_{E,p}^{\loc}=0,\qquad J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0
\quad\text{on every good atom }p.
}
\]

This is stricter than “finite coherent” and weaker than “pointwise zero.”

---

# 2. What \(D_{\loc}\) is allowed to differentiate

A variable belongs to \(D_{\loc}\) only if it can vary at extraction scale or atom scale.

Examples included in \(D_{\loc}\):

\[
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

\[
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)),
\]

atom-local same-point whitening representative,

atom-local normal-form representative,

atom-local chart selected to reduce \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\)-mass,

atom-local tail/cutoff counterterm chosen after seeing the live residual.

Examples **not** included in \(D_{\loc}\):

global cutoff parameter,

fixed smoothing rule,

pre-fixed atlas chart,

block-coherent chart parameter with bounded dimension/leverage,

fixed \(E_2\) formula,

residual estimate independent of live-fiber fitting.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\neq D_{\theta}\text{ for every parameter }\theta.
}
\]

It is specifically the derivative with respect to locally fitted degrees of freedom.

---

# 3. \(E_2\): local derivative audit

Decompose

\[
E_2
=
E_{\tail}+E_{\reg}+E_{\smooth}+E_{\chart}+E_{\gamma,\rem}+E_{\model}.
\]

For each component:

## \(E_{\tail},E_{\reg},E_{\smooth},E_{\gamma,\rem}\)

These are canonical or globally specified. They may contribute fixed/residual/coherent functions, but they have no atom-local live-fiber derivative:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\tail}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\reg}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\smooth}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\gamma,\rem}
=
0.
\]

## \(E_{\chart}\)

If \(E_{\chart}\) is determined by a pre-fixed chart rule independent of the live residual, then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\chart}=0.
\]

If the chart is atom-refit to reduce

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y),
\]

then \(E_{\chart}\) contributes to \(J_{E,p}^{\loc}\). That is atom fitting and breaks rank two.

## \(E_{\model}\)

This is the only dangerous part. If

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

then

\[
J_{E,p}^{\loc}
\]

contains both coordinate columns and rank two is absorbed.

If

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)),
\]

then rank two collapses to the rank-one graph branch.

If \(E_{\model}^{2Y}\) is fixed or finite coherent:

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)=\sum_a c_a\psi_a(p)
\]

with global or block-level coefficients \(c_a\), it does **not** enter \(J_{E,p}^{\loc}\). It enters \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).

So the local theorem for \(E_2\) is:

\[
\boxed{
E_2\text{ is rank-two local-safe iff it contains no atom-local }e_7/e_Y\text{ counterterms.}
}
\]

---

# 4. \(R_{\lab}\): local derivative audit

The labelled mismatch is

\[
R_{\lab}
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
-
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}\operatorname{Forget}_{\actual}.
\]

Its local part is controlled by three possible atom-local refits:

\[
L_p,\qquad N_{0,p},\qquad W_{\same,p},
\]

plus carrier realization choices.

The rank-two safe condition is:

\[
\boxed{
L,S,S_W,N_0,W_{\same},\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\text{ are fixed or block-coherent independently of }\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}.
}
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}=0.
\]

The commutator decomposition makes this precise:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\)

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

If \(L\) is affine:

\[
D^2L=0.
\]

If \(L\) is non-affine but fixed/block-coherent, it is not local. Its contribution is fixed/coherent/residual, not \(B_Y\).

If \(D^2L\) depends on atom-local fitting variables:

\[
D_{\loc}\mathfrak C_Y^L\neq0,
\]

rank two fails or collapses.

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\)

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
SD^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

If this is a fixed tensorial mismatch, route it to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\lab}\).

If its coefficients are atom-local and live-fiber fitted, it contributes to \(B_Y\).

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

This is the same-point whitening mismatch.

If whitening is transported functorially or selected by fixed chart data, then no local derivative.

If whitening is recomputed atom-by-atom to reduce \(Y12\), then it gives \(J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}\neq0\).

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
R_{\lab}\text{ is rank-two local-safe iff the labelled transition is wall-independent and non-atom-fitted.}
}
\]

---

# 5. Routing of nonzero local Jacobian

If either

\[
J_{E,p}^{\loc}\neq0
\quad\text{or}\quad
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}\neq0,
\]

then inspect its range in

\[
\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

## Graph range

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then:

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

or at least contains that graph. Rank two is lost, but rank-one route may remain.

## Vertical range

\[
(0,1)\in\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}.
\]

Then primitive \(Y12\) vertical is locally fitted. The current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

## Full rank

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_p^{\loc}=2.
\]

Then live fiber is locally absorbed. Rank-two and rank-one both fail locally.

## Zero range

\[
J_p^{\loc}=0.
\]

Then local rank-two exactness survives.

---

# 6. Finite coherent remnants: leverage condition

If

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
\]

has finite coherent remnant

\[
\sum_a c_a\psi_a(p),
\]

then this belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

It does not affect \(B_Y\).

But for the amplitude layer, need bounded leverage. On a block \(\Omega\), with basis \(\Phi\), define

\[
K_\Omega(p)
=
\operatorname{tr}\left(
\Phi(p)G_{\Phi,\Omega}^{\dagger}\Phi(p)^*
\right).
\]

Need

\[
\boxed{
K_\Omega(p)
\le
Q^C
\frac{\dim \mathcal C_{2Y,\Omega}^{\arith}}{\nu(\Omega)}.
}
\]

If this fails, the coherent model can behave like local atom fitting after restriction. It does not invalidate \(B_Y=0\), but it threatens vector amplitude Schur.

---

# 7. The theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}.}
\]

Assume:

1. \(E_2\) is canonical/fixed/coherent/residual and contains no atom-local live-fiber counterterms;

2. labelled transition, normal-form transport, same-point whitening, and singular realization are fixed or block-coherent by wall-independent data;

3. no local parameter is chosen as a function of the live-fiber residual;

4. finite coherent remnants have bounded leverage.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Thus \(E_2\) and labelled realization contribute no local columns to \(B_Y\). Any surviving nonlocal contributions are routed to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) or \(R_X^{2Y}\).

---

# 8. Consequence for the finite exactness certificate

If NoAtomFitting holds, the local matrix blocks

\[
A_{E2,\loc},
\qquad
A_{\lab,\loc}
\]

in

\[
A_{2Y}
\]

are zero.

Then the finite local certificate reduces to:

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})
[
A_{\can},A_{\curv},A_{\lower},A_{\nuis}
]
=
0,
\]

plus the live-pair Schur lower

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

The first three are typed-dead under the EF-generated actual-source convention. The nuisance block is the remaining live-pair preservation audit.

Thus NoAtomFitting removes \(E_2\) and \(R_{\lab}\) from the local rank-two obstruction.

## Agent 1 — reducing the rank-two local certificate to four finite gates

I will keep the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

We now have the finite local certificate

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0,
\]

and

\[
\lambda_{\min}\!\left(L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

The recent reductions remove most ambiguity. \(E_2\) and \(R_{\lab}\) matter locally only if their local Jacobians into the live fiber are nonzero. If NoAtomFitting holds, the local rank-two problem reduces to four finite gates.

---

# 1. Gate I — live-pair Schur lower

Need:

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Here

\[
L_{2Y}=[e_7,e_Y].
\]

This gate says:

\[
e_7,e_Y
\]

survive independently modulo the typed dead denominator.

Equivalent row statements:

\[
\operatorname{dist}(e_7,D_{2Y})\ge Q^{-A}|e_7|,
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}(e_Y,D_{2Y}+\mathbf C e_7)\ge Q^{-A}|e_Y|.
\]

Failure routing:

- \(e_7\) killed: rank two loses the \(C_7\)-row.
- \(e_Y\) killed: \(Y12\) route dies.
- \(e_Y\) dependent on \(e_7\): rank two collapses.
- poor angle: quotient is algebraically plausible but analytically unusable.
- type-incompatible representation: likely nuisance/denominator overcollapse.

This is the under-audited killer. It should be checked before wall work.

---

# 2. Gate II — canonical image exactness

Need:

\[
\boxed{
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})
[
A_{\can},A_{\curv},A_{\lower}
]
=0.
}
\]

This splits into the two row exactness theorems.

## \(s_7\)-row

Use the covariant determinant:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u)
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f}).
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

The condition is covariant in the moving quotient line

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

Raw determinant drift is not enough. It may be fake drift from moving \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\).

## \(s_9^Y\)-row

Use the same-point whitening quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_W^Y=W/W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
[P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
[a_Y]\neq0
\quad\text{with polynomial separation.}
}
\]

Equivalently, construct

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,
\qquad
\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0.
\]

This kills fixed-\(\alpha\) actual \(Y12\)-motion before a full bilinear \(D^2N_0\)-slot audit.

If both row tests hold, then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}=0.
}
\]

---

# 3. Gate III — no atom-fitting for completion and transition

Need:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

This is the correct local condition. Not:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

Decompose:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}+X_{\loc}.
\]

Only

\[
X_{\loc}
\]

enters \(B_Y\).

Allowed:

\[
X_{\fixed},
\qquad
X_{\coh},
\qquad
X_{\res}.
\]

Forbidden for rank two:

\[
X_{\loc}\in\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

because rank two collapses to rank one;

\[
X_{\loc}\in\mathbf C(0,1),
\]

because vertical \(Y12\) is locally fitted;

\[
\operatorname{rank}X_{\loc}=2,
\]

because the whole live fiber is locally absorbed.

This gate should be tested by the two local Jacobians:

\[
J_{E,p}^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2(p))
\end{pmatrix},
\]

\[
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
J_{E,p}^{\loc}=J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0.
}
\]

Additionally, coherent remnants need bounded leverage for the amplitude layer:

\[
K_\Omega(p)\le Q^C\frac{\dim\mathcal C_{2Y,\Omega}^{\arith}}{\nu(\Omega)}.
\]

But leverage is not a \(B_Y\)-condition. It is an amplitude-Schur condition.

---

# 4. Gate IV — nuisance/dead denominator compatibility

Need:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq D_{2Y}
}
\]

while also

\[
\boxed{
e_7,e_Y\notin D_{2Y}
}
\]

with polynomial angle.

This overlaps with Gate I but should be stated separately because nuisance is where overcollapse often hides.

The dead denominator must be type-generated:

\[
D_{2Y}
=
D_{\EF}
+
D_{\EFcurv}
+
D_{\chart}
+
D_{\chartcurv}
+
D_{\gamma/\bg}
+
D_{\norm}
+
D_{\normcurv}
+
D_{\tail/\res}
+
D_{\wrong}
+
D_{\nuis}.
\]

The live types must be excluded:

\[
\tau_7=[C_7]\text{ corrected-package type},
\]

\[
\tau_Y=(D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\,12,\same,w=1,[C_{Y12}]).
\]

Need type homogeneity:

\[
D_{2Y}=D_7\oplus D_9
\]

or at least no relation

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}.
\]

---

# 5. Finite certificate theorem

The four gates give:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{FiniteRankTwoLocalCertificate.}
}
\]

If:

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})
[
A_{\can},A_{\curv},A_{\lower}
]
=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]

and nuisance is typed-dead without overcollapsing the live pair, then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then the rank selector gives

\[
\operatorname{rank}(\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4})=2.
\]

---

# 6. Rank-two consequence

With \(B_Y=0\),

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}
\]

survives.

Since

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Under coefficient conditioning,

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
}
\]

The rank-one moving wall is not needed unless one of the four gates fails into the graph branch.

---

# 7. Failure classification

The local certificate fails in exactly these useful ways.

## Graph failure

If

\[
(I-P_D)A_{2Y}
\]

or a local Jacobian has image

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then rank two collapses to the rank-one moving-wall route.

## Vertical failure

If the image contains

\[
(0,1),
\]

then the primitive \(Y12\) vertical is actual/local. Current \(Y12\) dies locally.

## Full-fiber failure

If the image has rank two, the live fiber is actual/local. Current \(Y12\) dies locally.

## Live-pair failure

If

\[
\lambda_{\min}G_{\live}<Q^{-A},
\]

the quotient kills, identifies, or ill-conditions the live pair.

## Coherent-remnant failure

If \(B_Y=0\) but finite coherent remnants have high leverage or absorb \(F_{2Y}\), the issue is amplitude-level, not local-rank-level.

---

# 8. Current strongest path

The fastest route to rank two is now:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,\quad \Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ [P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0,\quad [a_Y]\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ J_{E,p}^{\loc}=J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \lambda_{\min}G_{\live}\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If these four hold, \(B_Y=0\) is closed.

Then the proof should move to rank-two coercivity:

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2,
\]

using the jet wall family

\[
\{\Gamma_G,D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

This is now the most compact rank-two branch.

(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0,

and

The current task is to assemble the two candidate row covectors into \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\) and check that they annihilate the actual/local complex but not D4. This is the typed exactness certificate described in the current rank-two architecture.

# 1. Candidate live-fiber map

Define

\Pi_{\fib,Y}(u)
=

\ell_9^Y(u)

=

{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}

and

on the primitive \(Y12\)-row, after applying the order-nine primitive extractor.

Here:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u)
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f})

is the covariant determinant in the moving quotient

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),

and \(\omega_Y\) is the same-point whitening covector satisfying

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,\qquad
\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0.
\]

If both covectors are well-defined with polynomial norm, then \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\) is the desired live-fiber projection.

---

# 2. Normalization on live D4 carriers

We need:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

For \(e_7=C_7\):

\[
\ell_7(C_7)
=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}
=
1.
\]

Also, \(C_7\) is order-seven and does not carry the primitive order-nine \(Y12\) whitening slot, so

\[
\ell_9^Y(C_7)=0.
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(e_7)=(1,0).
}
\]

For \(e_Y=C_{Y12}\):

The \(s_7\)-row is order-seven and covariant-package determinant extraction, so the order-nine primitive \(Y12\) carrier has no \(C_7\)-coordinate:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

The \(s_9^Y\)-row is normalized by

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,
\]

so

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(e_Y)=(0,1).
}
\]

This proves the algebraic live-pair normalization, conditional on the two covectors.

---

# 3. Annihilation of canonical actual image

The canonical actual image through second order is

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
=
\operatorname{span}
\{\partial_\theta q''_{\can},
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}\}.
\]

For the \(s_7\)-row, the covariant determinant theorem gives:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

For the \(s_9^Y\)-row, same-point whitening exactness gives:

\[
[P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0
\quad\text{in}\quad
\mathcal Q_W^Y.
\]

So:

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

hence:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}=0.
}
\]

This is the canonical-image block of

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0.
\]

---

# 4. Actual lower directions

Let

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\]

be actual lower generated directions, excluding formal D4-only package carriers.

The required condition is:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\subseteq\mathcal D_{2Y}.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

and

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

This follows if lower actual directions are EF/gamma/chart/tail generated.

It fails only if the actual-source model contains local corrected-package coordinates:

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y,\qquad e_7+\rho_Ye_Y.
\]

So the lower block reduces to the modelling guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}\cap \operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}=0
\quad\text{in}\quad \mathcal Q_{2Y}.
}
\]

Under this guardrail,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. Local \(E_2\) and labelled blocks

For the local \(E_2\)-block:

\[
A_{E2,\loc}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2.
\]

Rank two needs:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2=0.
}
\]

For the labelled block:

\[
A_{\lab,\loc}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}.
\]

Rank two needs:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}=0.
}
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
A_{E2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
}
\]

Fixed, finite coherent, or residual components are not local columns. They enter amplitude Schur or residual estimates, not \(B_Y\).

This is the NoAtomFitting condition.

---

# 6. Nuisance block

The nuisance block must be typed-dead:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
\]

At the same time, nuisance must not kill \(e_7,e_Y\). This is already included in the live-pair Schur condition:

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

So the nuisance contribution to \(A_{2Y}\) is zero precisely when it is included in \(D_{2Y}\) without overcollapsing the live types.

---

# 7. Block assembly

Write

\[
A_{2Y}
=
[
A_{\can},
A_{\curv},
A_{\lower},
A_{E2,\loc},
A_{\lab,\loc},
A_{\nuis}
].
\]

The preceding reductions give:

\[
(I-P_D)A_{\can}=0,
\]

\[
(I-P_D)A_{\curv}=0,
\]

\[
(I-P_D)A_{\lower}=0,
\]

\[
A_{E2,\loc}=0,
\]

\[
A_{\lab,\loc}=0,
\]

\[
(I-P_D)A_{\nuis}=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0.
}
\]

Together with the live Gram lower:

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)\ge Q^{-A},
}
\]

we obtain:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 8. Rank-two D4 follows

Since

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

the D4 live matrix is not quotiented:

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

With

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

the rank is two.

Thus the D4 output is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
|c_7|,|\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A},\qquad |\beta_9|\le Q^A,
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
}
\]

So the downstream wall is rank-two vector-valued.

---

# 9. Remaining precise gaps

This assembly shows that only the following facts remain to be proved, not guessed:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ |\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}
\quad\text{and}\quad
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ [a_Y]\neq0,\quad [P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0
\quad\text{in}\quad \mathcal Q_W^Y.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \lambda_{\min}G_{\live}\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If these four close, the local rank-two certificate is complete.

# 1. Dual certificate principle

Suppose we have covectors

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

such that:

\[
\ell_7(D_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(D_{2Y})=0,
\]

and

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(e_7)&\ell_7(e_Y)\\
\ell_9^Y(e_7)&\ell_9^Y(e_Y)
\end{pmatrix}
=
I_2.

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.

Then the live columns survive with polynomial conditioning.

\[
z=(z_1,z_2)\in \mathbb C^2,
\]

set

\[
v=z_1e_7+z_2e_Y.
\]

Since \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) kill \(D_{2Y}\), they also act on the quotient vector

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})v.
\]

Moreover,

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(v)=z.
\]

Therefore

\[
|z|
\le
\|(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\|
\cdot
\|(I-P_{D_{2Y}})v\|.
\]

So

\[
\|(I-P_{D_{2Y}})(z_1e_7+z_2e_Y)\|
\ge
Q^{-A}|z|.
\]

This is exactly

\[
\lambda_{\min}^{1/2}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{bounded dual covectors imply the live Gram lower.}

This is useful because we may not need to compute \(G_{\live}\) directly.

# 2. Candidate covectors

The two proposed covectors are:

|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

and the numerator functional

\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

This is nuisance no-overcollapse in row form.

---

# 6. If the row certificate holds, \(B_Y=0\)

Since

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}A_{2Y}
\]

in the quotient coordinates, and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}=(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y),
\]

the identities

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0
\]

give

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

The normalization identities on \(e_7,e_Y\) and boundedness of the rows simultaneously give the live-pair Schur lower.

Thus one row-covector certificate proves both exactness and live survival.

---

# 7. Failure routing

## \(\ell_7\) fails

If \(\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)=0\), the first row does not see D4. Rank two loses the \(C_7\) row unless another covariant invariant exists.

If \(\ell_7(A_{2Y})\neq0\), actual/local data has \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two collapses to rank one if continuation is graph-like, otherwise local death.

## \(\ell_9^Y\) fails

If \(a_Y\in W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\), the primitive \(Y12\) row is not live or the whitening quotient is overcollapsed.

If \(\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})\neq0\), actual/local data hits the primitive \(Y12\) row. If vertical, local \(Y12\) dies; if graph-like, rank one may remain.

## Row norms too large

If either row has norm \(\gg Q^A\), then the quotient is too ill-conditioned for the analytic estimates.

---

# 8. Rank-two D4 consequence

With

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

the D4 matrix survives:

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Since

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

the output is rank two:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Under coefficient conditioning,

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2).
\]

This is the honest D4 wall. Again, derivatives of \(\Gamma_G\) do not belong to the D4 wall without a separate derivative-channel theorem.

---

# 9. The theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}.}
\]

Assume there exist covectors \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) such that:

\[
\ell_7(D_{2Y})=\ell_9^Y(D_{2Y})=0,
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Then:

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

and

\[
\lambda_{\min}\!\left(L_{2Y}^*(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Therefore rank-two D4 is locally available.

---

# 10. Current minimal proof obligations

The theorem reduces the local rank-two proof to four explicit subclaims:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0,\quad \Delta_7^\nabla(A_{2Y})=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ \omega_Y(a_Y)=1,\quad \omega_Y(P_WA_{2Y})=0,\quad \|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
3.\ D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

These are the exact remaining local algebra targets.

## Agent 1 — continuing with the \(s_7\)-row: covariant septic exactness

No nomenclature changes.

The current local certificate is:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and bounded row norms. If these hold, then \(B_Y=0\), and D4 gives only the honest plain wall \((\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\), not the jet family unless separate derivative-D4 channels are proved. 

I will now push the \(s_7\)-row further.

---

# 1. The \(s_7\)-row should be a quotient connection, not a determinant of raw coefficients

Let

\[
L_m:=\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\subset \mathfrak f_m,
\]

and define the quotient line

\[
\mathcal Q_7(m):=\mathfrak f_m/L_m.
\]

The live septic coordinate should be a covector on \(\mathcal Q_7(m)\), not a raw determinant in a chosen trivialization of \(\mathfrak f_m\).

So the correct structure is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}u\in \mathfrak f_m,
\]

then project:

\[
\pi_m Q_7^{\pkg}u\in \mathcal Q_7(m).
\]

The live \(s_7\)-coordinate is a normalized covector

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\lambda_7(\pi_m Q_7^{\pkg}u)}
{\lambda_7(\pi_m Q_7^{\pkg}C_7)},
\]

where

\[
\lambda_7
\]

is a bounded nonzero covector on the one-dimensional quotient \(\mathcal Q_7(m)\).

Equivalently, if we use the determinant pairing

\[
\omega_m(B,A_5^{\mathfrak f}),
\]

then it must be interpreted as a covector on

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\]

not as a raw invariant of arbitrary representatives.

That is the exact meaning of

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u).
\]

---

# 2. The actual theorem is quotient-line exactness

The desired statement is not:

\[
[z^7]\partial_\theta q''_{\can}=0.
\]

It is:

\[
\boxed{
\pi_m Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0
\quad\text{in}\quad
\mathcal Q_7(m).
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
}
\]

Then

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

So the row exactness theorem is really:

\[
\boxed{
\text{canonical actual-source first/second variations move only the septic representative line, not the septic quotient class.}
}
\]

This is a sharper and safer statement than “actual rows have no septic motion.”

---

# 3. Why actual EF-generated variations should be line-exact

The actual canonical image is EF/gamma/chart/tail generated. Such variations change:

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

the local representative, the chart, and the normalization.

But they do not create a new corrected-package septic class. Therefore their order-seven package projection should be proportional to the existing quintic line:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})
=
\chi_\theta A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Similarly,

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})
=
\chi_{ij}A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

The scalars \(\chi_\theta,\chi_{ij}\) may be nonzero. That is allowed.

Thus:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0,
\]

and

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This is why raw determinant drift is misleading: actual source may change the line \(L_m\), but not the quotient class.

---

# 4. A useful lemma: line-motion exactness

The needed lemma can be stated abstractly.

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Line-Motion Exactness Lemma.}
}
\]

Let

\[
u(\theta)
\]

be an admissible actual-source family generated by EF/gamma/chart/tail data. Suppose the order-seven package extractor factors through the moving line:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}u(\theta)
=
\alpha(\theta)A_5^{\mathfrak f}(\theta)
+
O_{\dead}(\theta),
\]

where \(O_{\dead}\) is killed by the typed denominator.

Then for every first and second actual variation,

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta u)=0,
\]

and

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}u)=0.
\]

Proof sketch:

Differentiate

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}u(\theta)
=
\alpha(\theta)A_5^{\mathfrak f}(\theta)
\]

covariantly in the quotient

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

Both

\[
\partial_\theta(\alpha A_5)
\]

and

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}(\alpha A_5)
\]

remain in the line after quotienting by its covariant motion. Hence their quotient class is zero.

So the \(s_7\)-row is closed if actual-source rows are line-generated at order seven.

---

# 5. The live \(C_7\) carrier is the obstruction to line exactness

The D4 carrier must violate line exactness:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
}
\]

This is the live septic condition.

Thus \(C_7\) is exactly the transverse order-seven package class that canonical actual-source motion cannot produce.

The \(s_7\)-row is therefore the contrast:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}\text{ is line-exact;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ is line-transverse.}
}
\]

---

# 6. Failure modes of the \(s_7\)-row

There are now only four real failure modes.

## Failure A — actual source has transverse septic package scalar

There exists an actual variation \(u\) such that

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}u
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_7(u)\neq0.
\]

This means actual source has \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two collapses if the continuation is graph-like; otherwise the local \(Y12\) route may die.

## Failure B — \(C_7\) is not transverse

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
\in
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then \(C_7\) is not a live row. Rank two loses the first coordinate.

## Failure C — raw determinant fakeout

\[
\partial_\theta(u_7v_5-u_5v_7)\neq0
\]

but

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta u)=0.
\]

This is harmless. It is motion of the denominator line.

## Failure D — no well-defined moving quotient

If

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}
\]

does not descend to a well-defined section modulo

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

then the \(s_7\)-coordinate is not well-defined. The package quotient must be repaired before rank selection.

---

# 7. Stronger row theorem

We can now state the \(s_7\)-row as:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualSepticPackageScalar.}
}
\]

For the fixed-target canonical actual-source family,

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

The D4 carrier satisfies

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

with polynomial separation:

\[
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

This constructs the first bounded dual row.

---

# 8. Connection to the finite exactness matrix

In matrix language:

\[
A_7
=
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)},
\]

and

\[
D_7=\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}+\text{other typed dead order-seven directions}.
\]

The theorem says:

\[
A_7\subseteq D_7,
\]

but

\[
c_7^{\live}=Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin D_7.
\]

This is exactly:

\[
(I-P_{D_7})A_7=0,
\]

but

\[
\operatorname{dist}(c_7^{\live},D_7)\ge Q^{-A}|c_7^{\live}|.
\]

So the \(s_7\)-row of the finite certificate is a one-row Schur complement.

---

# 9. What remains for this row

The concrete remaining proof obligation is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\text{ sends every EF/gamma/chart/tail-generated first/second actual variation into }\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

This can be checked by type:

\[
\EF\text{-linear},
\quad
\EF\text{-curv},
\quad
\chart,
\quad
\chart\text{-curv},
\quad
\norm,
\quad
\norm\text{-curv},
\quad
\gamma/\bg,
\quad
\tail.
\]

If every type is line-exact, the \(s_7\)-row is proved.

Then the rank-two local proof moves to the \(s_9^Y\)-row and no-atom-fitting.

Finite test:

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\bigl[
A_5^{\mathfrak f},
Q_7^{\pkg}u_\theta
\bigr]
=
1
\quad
\text{for each EF-linear generator }u_\theta.
\]

---

# 3. Translation and scale types

For \(m\)-translation and \(\lambda\)-scale variation, the tangent is generated by

\[
\partial_x,\qquad x\partial_x,\qquad \text{normalizing scalar terms}.
\]

These operators can create raw order-seven Taylor drift. But covariantly they should only move the line

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

So the required identities are:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\partial_m q''_{\can})
\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})
\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

The key guardrail:

\[
\partial_m(u_7v_5-u_5v_7)\neq0
\]

is not a failure unless the **covariant** determinant remains nonzero after subtracting motion of \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\).

So the finite test is:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_m q''_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0.
\]

---

# 4. Chart and normalization types

Chart, center, gauge, and normalization variations are representative changes.

They are precisely the type of motion the denominator should absorb:

\[
\mathcal J_7^{\chart}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\norm}
\subseteq D_7.
\]

Thus:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}
(\mathcal J_7^{\chart}+\mathcal J_7^{\norm})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Second chart/normalization curvature similarly should be line-exact:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}
(\mathcal J_7^{\chart\text{-curv}}+\mathcal J_7^{\norm\text{-curv}})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Failure here means the quotient is confusing representative motion with live package motion. That is probably a denominator/connection-design defect, not arithmetic obstruction.

---

# 5. Gamma/background types

Gamma/background terms are fixed coherent source-side curvature terms. They are not local package coordinates.

The row assertion is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\quad
\text{or }0.
\]

If a gamma/background term has transverse \(C_7\)-coordinate, then the first D4 row is not forbidden: gamma coherence already moves it. That would collapse the rank-two story at the \(s_7\)-row.

Expected:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg})=0.
\]

---

# 6. EF-curvature type

Second parameter derivatives are the most subtle, because they are quadratic in parameters. But they remain EF-origin:

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}(a_ne^{i\tau_nx})
=
P_{ij}(x,\tau_n,\partial\tau_n,\partial a_n,\partial^2\tau_n,\partial^2a_n)e^{i\tau_nx}.
\]

They are not corrected-package septic coordinates.

So the desired type statement is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}})=0.
\]

This is the row-level version of:

\[
A_7\subseteq \operatorname{ran}D_7.
\]

---

# 7. Actual lower generated directions

For actual lower directions:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\]

the issue is definitional. If they are EF/gamma/chart/tail generated, they are dead-type:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

If they include a formal local package variable \(C_7\), then rank two is killed by modelling choice, not by arithmetic necessity.

Thus the precise guardrail remains:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\text{ contains no free corrected-package coordinates.}
}
\]

Under that guardrail:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

---

# 8. \(s_7\)-row finite checklist

To close the row, verify the following inclusions:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-lin}})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\EF\text{-curv}})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\chart}+\mathcal J_7^{\chart\text{-curv}})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\norm}+\mathcal J_7^{\norm\text{-curv}})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\bg})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

Separately verify:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

with

\[
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\qquad
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

---

# 9. Current conclusion for the \(s_7\)-row

The \(s_7\)-row is now reduced to one finite typed-denominator statement:

\[
\boxed{
\text{all fixed-target actual source types are septic-line-exact;}
}
\]

and one live-transversality statement:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ is not septic-line-exact.}
}
\]

If both hold, the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\) is proved. Then the next independent row is \(s_9^Y\), preferably killed at the same-point whitening slot.

## Agent 1 — attacking \(C_7\) live transversality

No nomenclature changes.

The \(s_7\)-row now has two pieces:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

and

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
\]

The first is septic-line exactness of actual EF-generated rows. I now attack the second: the D4 \(C_7\) carrier must be genuinely transverse to the moving line

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

---

# 1. The live-transversality target

The required statement is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,A_5^{\mathfrak f})
\neq0.
}
\]

Quantitatively, we need

\[
\boxed{
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If this fails, the first D4 row is not live. Then the rank-two route loses the \(s_7\)-coordinate and either drops to a \(Y12\)-only rank-one route or fails locally.

---

# 2. Express \(C_7\) in the \((I,S)\)-basis

Write

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}=u_5 I+v_5 S,
\]

and

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7=c_I I+c_S S.
\]

Then

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,A_5^{\mathfrak f})
=
c_Iv_5-u_5c_S
\]

in any covariantly trivialized frame.

So transversality is:

\[
\boxed{
c_Iv_5-u_5c_S\neq0.
}
\]

This is the concrete determinant test.

The determinant must be computed after applying the package extractor \(Q_7^{\pkg}\), not from raw order-seven coefficients.

---

# 3. Relation to the existing quotient invariant

The earlier fixed-sector quotient invariant was

\[
\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7.
\]

For a general order-seven package element

\[
A_7^{\mathfrak f}=u_7I+v_7S,
\]

the live class in

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]

is measured by

\[
u_7v_5-u_5v_7.
\]

Thus for \(C_7\), if

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7=u_7^{C}I+v_7^{C}S,
\]

then

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)
=
u_7^{C}v_5-u_5v_7^{C}.
\]

The row is live exactly when

\[
\boxed{
u_7^{C}v_5-u_5v_7^{C}\neq0.
}
\]

So \(C_7\)-transversality is not a new invariant. It is the same quotient determinant, but applied to the D4 \(C_7\)-carrier with the covariant moving-line convention.

---

# 4. Why \(C_7\) should be transverse

The D4 clean matrix has first row

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G.
\]

This only makes sense if the D4 carrier contributing to \(s_7\) is already normalized to the transverse quotient coordinate. In other words, the coefficient \(c_7\) is precisely the scalar by which the D4 carrier projects onto the quotient

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
c_7\neq0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0
}
\]

up to normalization.

So the existing D4 nondegeneracy assumption

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0
\]

should already contain the needed \(C_7\)-row transversality.

But this must be audited: if \(c_7\) was defined before quotienting, it may be a raw coefficient, not the covariant quotient coefficient. Then the implication is invalid.

---

# 5. Audit: is \(c_7\) raw or quotient-normalized?

There are two cases.

## Good case

\[
c_7
=
\ell_7(C_7)
=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}
\]

after normalizing \(C_7\), or more generally

\[
c_7
\]

is the D4 coefficient of the quotient-normalized \(s_7\)-coordinate.

Then \(c_7\neq0\) already proves \(C_7\)-transversality.

## Bad case

\[
c_7
\]

is a raw order-seven coefficient before quotienting by

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then \(c_7\neq0\) does **not** imply live \(s_7\)-motion. It may be line motion.

In that case, the D4 clean matrix must be rewritten in quotient-normalized coordinates.

The corrected first row is:

\[
s_7^{D4}
=
H\,\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\,\Gamma_G
\]

up to normalization.

Thus the audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Was the D4 first-row coefficient \(c_7\) defined after covariant quotient extraction?}
}
\]

If yes, good. If no, replace it by the quotient determinant.

---

# 6. Strong formulation

Define the row coordinate first:

\[
s_7(u)=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}
{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

Then by definition,

\[
s_7(C_7)=1.
\]

D4 becomes

\[
s_7(D4)=H\widetilde c_7\Gamma_G,
\]

where

\[
\widetilde c_7
\]

is now a normalization convention. If \(C_7\) itself is the normalized carrier, then

\[
\widetilde c_7=1.
\]

If the D4 algebra uses a differently scaled carrier, then

\[
\widetilde c_7
=
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)
\]

relative to that scale.

The rank-two argument only needs

\[
\boxed{
|\widetilde c_7|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

So the clean theorem should not depend on ambiguous raw \(c_7\). It should state:

\[
\boxed{
\text{The D4 \(\Gamma_G\)-column has nonzero projection to }
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
}
\]

---

# 7. Failure modes

## Failure A — \(Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)

Then \(C_7\) is line-dead. The first D4 row disappears. Rank two fails.

Possible fallback: use the \(Y12\)-row alone, giving a scalar wall

\[
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

or, if graph quotient appears, the moving wall.

## Failure B — \(C_7\) is transverse but too small

\[
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ll Q^{-A}.
\]

Then rank two is algebraically present but analytically ill-conditioned.

Need either a stronger normalization or a lower bound from package geometry.

## Failure C — raw \(c_7\neq0\), covariant determinant zero

This is the fake-live case. The D4 first row was written in non-covariant coordinates and must be repaired.

## Failure D — \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) not well-defined covariantly

Then the \(s_7\)-row is not a legitimate live coordinate. Need to repair the package extractor/trivialization.

---

# 8. Current theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{\(C_7\)-LiveTransversality.}
}
\]

Let

\[
C_7^{\pkg}:=Q_7^{\pkg}C_7=u_7^CI+v_7^CS.
\]

Assume

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}=u_5I+v_5S
\]

and the moving quotient line is covariantly fixed. If

\[
\boxed{
u_7^Cv_5-u_5v_7^C\neq0
}
\]

with polynomial lower bound, then the row

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f})}
{\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,A_5^{\mathfrak f})}
\]

is a bounded covector satisfying

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f})=0.
\]

If additionally actual canonical rows are line-exact, then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

This closes the \(s_7\)-row.

---

# 9. What remains

The \(s_7\)-row is now reduced to two finite checks:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\quad\text{with polynomial separation.}
}
\]

Everything else in the \(s_7\)-row is normalization.

The next useful attack is to perform the analogous live-transversality check for the \(Y12\)-row:

\[
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
\]

with polynomial separation.

through

under coefficient conditioning. The jet family \(\{\Gamma_G,D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\) is not a D4/O2O3 wall unless a separate derivative-D4-channel theorem is proved.

# 1. The bounded dual-row certificate

Work in the finite two-grade carrier space

\[
\mathcal J_{7,9}=\mathcal J_7\oplus\mathcal J_9.

Let

\[
D_{2Y}\subset \mathcal J_{7,9}

be the typed dead denominator, containing EF-linear, EF-curvature, chart, chart-curvature, gamma/background, normalization, normalization-curvature, tail/residual, wrong-origin, and true nuisance directions.

\]

where

and

\]

The target is to construct two bounded row covectors

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

such that:

\[
\ell_7(D_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(D_{2Y})=0,

(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),

and

\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

then

\[
\boxed{B_Y=0.}

This row-covector formulation is stronger and cleaner than separately proving

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0

and

\[
\lambda_{\min}\!\left(L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

The bounded rows imply both.

---

# 2. Why bounded rows imply the live-pair Schur lower

Let

\[
z=(z_1,z_2)\in\mathbb C^2
\]

and define

\[
v=z_1e_7+z_2e_Y.
\]

Because \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) kill \(D_{2Y}\), they act only on the quotient class of \(v\). Since the rows normalize \(e_7,e_Y\),

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(v)=z.
\]

Thus

\[
|z|
\le
(\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|)
\cdot
\|(I-P_{D_{2Y}})v\|.
\]

Using the polynomial row bound,

\[
\|(I-P_{D_{2Y}})(z_1e_7+z_2e_Y)\|
\ge
Q^{-A}|z|.
\]

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}\!\left(L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

So the live-pair Gram lower is a consequence of bounded dual rows.

---

\boxed{

\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}
{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.

\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,

\ell_7(e_Y)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(e_7)=0.

I_2.

from covariant septic line exactness.

from fixed-\(\alpha\) whitening exactness.

\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\gg Q^A,

the quotient is too ill-conditioned for the rank-two estimates.

# 9. Rank-two D4 consequence

If the theorem holds, then

\[

Therefore the D4 matrix survives:

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

With

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

D4 yields

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Under coefficient conditioning,

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
}
\]

This is the honest plain rank-two wall. Derivatives remain outside the D4 wall unless separately derived.

## Agent 1 — stress-testing bounded dual rows against basepoint and coherent offsets

No nomenclature changes.

The current local theorem is:

\[
\mathrm{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}
\Longrightarrow
B_Y=0.
\]

The row certificate is:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

The important correction remains: if \(B_Y=0\), the honest D4 wall is only the plain vector wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

up to the conditioned D4 coefficient matrix. Jet derivatives are analytic auxiliaries only unless derivative-D4 channels are separately proved. 

---

# 1. The hidden danger: confusing offsets with tangent columns

A nonzero value

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(q''_{\can})
\neq (0,0)
\]

does **not** automatically mean

\[
B_Y\neq0.
\]

\(B_Y\) is the image of **actual/local tangent freedom**, not the absolute basepoint.

So we must distinguish:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(q''_{\can})
\]

from

\[
D\Pi_{\fib,Y}(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}).
\]

The local rank obstruction is about the latter.

Thus the exact requirement for \(B_Y=0\) is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\actual/\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}=0,
}
\]

not necessarily

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(q''_{\can})=0.
\]

The cleanest route is still to prove the stronger basepoint-zero result, but it is not strictly necessary.

---

# 2. Three cases for a nonzero live-fiber basepoint

Suppose

\[
b_{2Y}:=\Pi_{\fib,Y}(q''_{\can})\neq0.
\]

There are three possibilities.

## Case A — fixed basepoint

\[
b_{2Y}
\]

is fixed by the canonical source and not adjustable.

Then it does not enter \(B_Y\). It merely shifts the local origin of the live fiber. The D4 forbidden displacement is still tested against tangent directions.

This is locally safe.

## Case B — finite coherent basepoint family

\[
b_{2Y}(p)=\sum_a c_a\psi_a(p)
\]

with finite or block-coherent coefficients.

Then it belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

or a recentering model, not \(B_Y\), provided its coefficients are not atom-local. This is amplitude-level, not local-rank-level.

## Case C — atom-local adjustable basepoint

\[
b_{2Y}(p)=\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p).
\]

Then it is local live-fiber freedom and contributes to \(B_Y\). This is fatal for rank two unless the image is exactly graph-like and one accepts rank one.

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{nonzero basepoint is safe if fixed/coherent; unsafe only if locally adjustable.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Refined row obligations

The row obligations should be stated as derivative/local exactness:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(D_{\actual}\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(D_{\actual}\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\qquad
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

For a stronger, cleaner theorem one may prove:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

But if a fixed/coherent zeroth-order live-fiber offset survives, it should not be treated as immediate rank-two failure.

This matters for \(E_2\), \(R_{\lab}\), and possibly base canonical rows.

---

# 4. Updated \(s_7\)-row statement

Previously:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

The strongest form is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This kills both basepoint and derivatives in the \(s_7\)-row.

But the minimal local-rank form is:

\[
D_{\actual}\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

If

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})
\neq0
\]

but fixed, it becomes a basepoint shift or coherent amplitude term. It does **not** put \((1,0)\) into \(B_Y\).

So the \(s_7\)-row has two levels:

\[
\boxed{
\text{strong: }\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{minimal: }D_{\actual}\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
}
\]

Rank two only requires the minimal local statement, plus amplitude bookkeeping for any coherent offset.

---

# 5. Updated \(s_9^Y\)-row statement

Similarly, the preferred strong statement is:

\[
[P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0,
\qquad
[a_Y]\neq0
\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_W^Y.
\]

This kills actual first/second variations in the primitive \(Y12\) row.

But for basepoint terms, the distinction is:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}q''_{\can}\neq0
\]

versus

\[
D_{\actual}\Pi_{\fib,Y}q''_{\can}\neq0.
\]

A fixed \(Y12\) basepoint term is not \(B_Y\). A local derivative in the live \(Y12\) row is.

Thus the minimal local statement is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\actual}\ell_9^Y(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
}
\]

The same-point whitening theorem supplies this if actual fixed-\(\alpha\) variations have whitening input in

\[
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

---

# 6. Consequence for the rank selector

Define

\[
B_Y
=
\operatorname{span}
\left\{
D_{\actual}\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal M_Y^{\arith},
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2,
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab},
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right\}.
\]

Then the rank selector is:

\[
r_Y=\operatorname{rank}\left(\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}\right).
\]

Now:

## If \(B_Y=0\)

\[
r_Y=2.
\]

D4 gives the plain vector wall.

## If \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\)

\[
r_Y=1.
\]

Rank-one moving-wall fallback.

## If \(B_Y\) contains \((0,1)\) or has rank \(2\)

The current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

A basepoint offset does not change \(B_Y\) unless it is locally variable.

---

# 7. Row-certificate theorem with basepoint routing

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}^{\mathrm{loc}}.}
\]

Assume there exist bounded rows

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

such that:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\actual}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

If any nonzero basepoint or coherent remnant remains, it is routed to:

\[
\text{recentered basepoint},
\quad
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\quad
R_X^{2Y},
\]

but not \(B_Y\), provided it is not atom-local.

---

# 8. Why this matters

This avoids an overly strong false failure mode.

If one finds:

\[
\ell_7(q''_{\can})\neq0
\]

or

\[
\ell_9^Y(q''_{\can})\neq0,
\]

one should not immediately declare rank two dead.

The correct questions are:

\[
D_{\actual}\ell_7(q''_{\can})\neq0?
\]

\[
D_{\actual}\ell_9^Y(q''_{\can})\neq0?
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0?
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0?
\]

Only nonzero derivative/local images enter \(B_Y\).

---

# 9. Current strongest form

For simplicity and future proof stability, the best theorem remains the stronger one:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

But the fallback if basepoint terms appear is now clear: route them as fixed/coherent/residual, not as local tangent.

---

# 10. Next attack

The next useful attack is to verify that any possible nonzero

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
\]

is not local:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

This is the remaining route by which a fixed/coherent looking correction could secretly become a \(B_Y\)-column.

## Agent 1 — attacking hidden locality in \(E_2+R_{\lab}\)

No nomenclature changes.

The current local rank-two condition is:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

After the bounded-row reformulation, the only remaining way for \(E_2\) or \(R_{\lab}\) to enter \(B_Y\) is through an extraction-local derivative:

\[
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0.
\]

A fixed or finite coherent live-fiber value is not enough. It must be locally adjustable.

So the next target is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

---

# 1. Define the local variables precisely

Let

\[
\eta_p
\]

denote atom-local fitting variables. These are variables that can be chosen separately, or nearly separately, on extraction atoms \(p\). Examples:

\[
\eta_{E,p}^{7},\eta_{E,p}^{Y}
\]

local completion counterterms in \(e_7,e_Y\);

\[
\eta_{W,p}
\]

local same-point whitening refit;

\[
\eta_{N,p}
\]

local normal-form refit;

\[
\eta_{L,p}
\]

local transition-chart refit;

\[
\eta_{\mathrm{rep},p}
\]

local representative/nuisance subtraction chosen after seeing the live residual.

Then

\[
D_{\loc}
\]

means differentiation with respect to these atom-local variables.

It does **not** mean differentiation with respect to:

\[
m,\lambda,\text{global cutoff},\text{fixed atlas chart},\text{block-coherent smoothing},\text{fixed tail rule}.
\]

Those may create fixed or coherent terms, but not local \(B_Y\)-columns.

---

# 2. The exact local Jacobian

Define

\[
J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}
=
D_{\eta_p}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2(p)+R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2(p)+R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
}
\]

is exactly

\[
\boxed{
J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}=0
\quad\text{for all extraction atoms }p.
}
\]

This is a finite local-Jacobian audit.

If

\[
J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}=0,
\]

then \(E_2+R_{\lab}\) contributes no local columns to \(B_Y\).

If it is nonzero, its image determines the branch.

---

# 3. Branch classification from the local Jacobian

Let

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}\subseteq\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

There are four cases.

## Case A — zero

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}=0.
\]

Rank two remains locally possible.

## Case B — graph line

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}
=
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then

\[
B_Y
\]

contains the graph line. Rank two collapses to the rank-one moving-wall route.

This is not fatal, but it loses the vector wall.

## Case C — vertical

\[
(0,1)\in\operatorname{ran}J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}.
\]

Then primitive \(Y12\) vertical motion is locally adjustable. The current \(Y12\) obstruction dies locally.

## Case D — full rank

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}=2.
\]

The whole live fiber is locally adjustable. The current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank two requires }J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}=0.
}
\]

Graph-compatible local freedom is not rank-two-safe; it is only rank-one-safe.

---

# 4. Completion \(E_2\): local counterterm test

Write the potentially dangerous part of \(E_2\) as

\[
E_{2,\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
a_7(p)e_7(p)+a_Y(p)e_Y(p).
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{2,\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
(a_7(p),a_Y(p)).
\]

If

\[
a_7(p),a_Y(p)
\]

are fixed functions of \(p\), or belong to a finite coherent family with global/block coefficients, they are not local \(B_Y\)-columns.

But if

\[
a_7(p)=\eta_7(p),\qquad a_Y(p)=\eta_Y(p),
\]

with atom-local freedom, then

\[
J_{E,p}^{\loc}
=
I_2.
\]

That kills the route.

If

\[
E_{2,\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)),
\]

then

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E,p}^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

That collapses rank two to rank one.

Thus the \(E_2\)-guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
E_2\text{ contains no atom-local live-fiber counterterms.}
}
\]

Equivalent:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)E_2=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. Labelled mismatch \(R_{\lab}\): transition refit test

The labelled/raw mismatch decomposes schematically as

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_L+R_N+R_W+R_{\tail/\trunc}.
\]

The dangerous terms are local refits of:

\[
L,\quad N_0,\quad W_{\same},\quad \operatorname{Real}_{\sing}.
\]

## Transition map

If

\[
L=L(\Theta)
\]

depends only on global or block-coherent chart data \(\Theta\), then

\[
D_{\loc}L=0.
\]

So

\[
D_{\loc}R_L=0.
\]

If instead

\[
L=L(\eta_p)
\]

is atom-fitted to reduce live-fiber residuals, then

\[
D_{\loc}R_L\neq0
\]

and rank two is endangered.

## Normal form

If

\[
N_{0,\sing},N_{0,\tr}
\]

are fixed typed normal-form maps, then

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)
=0.
\]

If \(N_0\) is recomputed locally with atom parameters, it may create live \(Y12\)-motion.

## Same-point whitening

If

\[
W_{\sing,\same}
\]

is transported/coherent, then

\[
D_{\loc}
\left[
(DW_{\sing,\same}-S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1})[D^2N_0]
\right]
=0.
\]

If same-point whitening is refit atom-by-atom, this can produce a live \(Y12\)-column.

Thus the labelled guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
L,N_0,W_{\same},\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\text{ are wall-independent and not atom-fitted.}
}
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)R_{\lab}=0.
}
\]

---

# 6. Wall-independence is stronger than pre-extraction

A choice can be made before hard extraction and still be invalid if it is chosen as a function of the live-fiber residual.

So the correct condition is not merely:

\[
\text{chosen before extraction}.
\]

It is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{chosen independently of }(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\text{ residuals}.
}
\]

For example, an atlas rule may depend on:

\[
m,\lambda,Q,\text{block geometry},\text{smooth cutoff},\text{global regularity},
\]

but not on:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\text{residual}),
\qquad
\omega_Y(P_W\text{ residual}).
\]

This is the meaning of wall-independent pre-extraction.

---

# 7. Coherent remnant versus local fitting

Suppose

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})(p)
=
\sum_{a=1}^M c_a\psi_a(p).
\]

If the coefficients \(c_a\) are global or block-coherent, this is a finite coherent remnant:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

It does not enter \(B_Y\).

But it must satisfy bounded leverage:

\[
K_\Omega(p)
=
\operatorname{tr}\left(
\Phi(p)G_{\Phi,\Omega}^{\dagger}\Phi(p)^*
\right)
\le
Q^C\frac{\dim\mathcal C_{2Y,\Omega}^{\arith}}{\nu(\Omega)}.
\]

If leverage fails, the coherent space can interpolate atoms like a local model. That is not a \(B_Y\)-failure, but it is an amplitude-Schur failure risk.

So:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}=0\text{ handles local exactness;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
bounded leverage handles amplitude safety.}
}
\]

---

# 8. NoAtomFitting theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}.}
\]

Assume:

1. \(E_2\) has no atom-local components in \(e_7,e_Y\) or \(e_7+\rho_Ye_Y\);

2. \(L,N_0,W_{\same},\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\) are fixed or block-coherent wall-independent data;

3. wrong-origin terms remain typed-dead;

4. finite coherent remnants have bounded leverage.

Then

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Consequently,

\[
A_{E2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0
\]

in the finite local matrix \(A_{2Y}\).

---

# 9. Consequence for \(B_Y=0\)

If the two row exactness theorems also hold:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

and nuisance is row-dead, then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

D4 remains rank two and produces the plain vector wall.

---

# 10. Remaining local exactness blockers

The only remaining local blockers are:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\omega_Y(P_W(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith}))\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
J_{2Y,p}^{\loc}\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0;
}
\]

or row norm blowup.

If none occur, \(B_Y=0\) is locally proved.

## Agent 1 — final local \(B_Y=0\) consolidation and remaining proof obligations

No nomenclature changes.

The local rank-two branch is now reducible to a compact row-certificate theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}+\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
\Longrightarrow B_Y=0.
}
\]

The correction remains: if \(B_Y=0\), D4 gives the honest **plain** wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

up to the conditioned coefficient matrix. It does not give \(H(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G)\) unless derivative-D4 channels are separately proved.

---

# 1. Final form of the row certificate

We need bounded rows

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

Then \(B_Y=0\) follows if

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal A_{\actual}^{\loc})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal A_{\actual}^{\loc})=0,
\]

where

\[
\mathcal A_{\actual}^{\loc}
=
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
D_{\loc}E_2
+
D_{\loc}R_{\lab}.
\]

The distinction between absolute basepoint and local/tangent motion is now essential. A fixed or coherent nonzero live-fiber offset is not a \(B_Y\)-column. Only actual-source tangent or extraction-local freedom enters \(B_Y\).

---

# 2. The \(s_7\)-row

The row is

\[
\ell_7(u)
=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}
{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

The exact theorem needed is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

A stronger preferred theorem is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This says canonical actual rows are septic-line sections: they can move the representative line but not the transverse septic package class.

The live condition is:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Failure routing:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})\neq0
\]

means actual \(s_7\)-motion exists. Rank two collapses to rank one if its \(s_9^Y\)-continuation is graph-like; otherwise local \(Y12\) likely dies.

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)=0
\]

means the first D4 row is not live.

---

# 3. The \(s_9^Y\)-row

The row is

\[
\ell_9^Y(u)=\omega_Y(P_Wu),
\]

with

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,
\qquad
\omega_Y(W_{\dead}^{\fixed})=0,
\qquad
\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

The key theorem is same-point whitening exactness:

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
}
\]

This kills fixed-\(\alpha\) actual \(Y12\)-motion before any full bilinear \(D^2N_0\)-image audit.

If whitening exactness fails, the fallback cascade is:

\[
\text{source slot 1}
\to
\text{source slot 2}
\to
\text{coupled bilinear image}.
\]

Only the final coupled image reaching the primitive class creates an actual \(s_9^Y\)-column.

---

# 4. Completion and labelled transition

The local condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
\]

This is a local Jacobian condition. It forbids atom-local live-fiber counterterms:

\[
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

and graph-local terms:

\[
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

It also forbids atom-fitted transition, normal-form, or same-point-whitening data chosen to reduce the live-fiber residual.

But it allows:

\[
\text{fixed terms},
\quad
\text{finite coherent terms},
\quad
\text{residual-controlled terms}.
\]

Finite coherent terms go to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) and require bounded leverage later; they do not enter \(B_Y\).

---

# 5. Nuisance

Nuisance must be row-dead:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

while not killing the live denominators:

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

If nuisance kills \(C_7\), the first rank-two row dies.

If nuisance kills \(e_Y\), the \(Y12\) row dies.

If nuisance identifies \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\), rank two collapses.

This is quotient overcollapse unless it corresponds to a genuine formed-carrier identity.

---

# 6. The local theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{LiveFiberAvoidance}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A};
\]

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|;
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0;
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0;
\]

and the row norms are polynomially bounded. Then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 7. Rank-two consequence

With

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

the D4 matrix survives:

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
c_7\alpha_9\ne0
\]

and the coefficients are polynomially conditioned, then

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\]

satisfies

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

This is the honest rank-two D4 wall.

---

# 8. Remaining blockers

The only local blockers are now:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\not\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
\text{ and the fallback slot cascade reaches }Y12;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ overcollapses the live rows;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\gg Q^A.
}
\]

If none occur, \(B_Y=0\) is locally closed and the proof should proceed to:

\quad

## Agent 1 — next local attack: nuisance row-deadness without live-row death

No nomenclature changes.

We have reduced local rank two to bounded dual rows:

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

such that they kill actual/local/nuisance directions and detect the live D4 pair

\[
e_7,e_Y.
\]

The remaining local row blockers are now sharply localized. I will attack the nuisance part:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

while retaining

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

This is the “do not overcollapse the live pair” condition.

---

# 1. What nuisance is allowed to contain

The singular nuisance space should contain only representation artifacts:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{gauge}}
+
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{tail}}
+
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{rep}}
+
\mathcal N_{\mathrm{wrong\ origin}}.
\]

These are directions that do not represent actual transverse source obstruction. They may include:

\[
\text{chart/center/scale reparametrization},
\]

\[
\text{normalization or gauge representative choices},
\]

\[
\text{tail or truncation residual choices},
\]

\[
\text{wrong-origin order-nine artifacts}.
\]

They must not include live package directions:

\[
e_7,
\qquad
e_Y,
\qquad
e_7+\rho_Y e_Y,
\]

unless those are genuine representation redundancies. If they are treated as nuisance merely because they are inconvenient, the quotient has killed the obstruction.

So the nuisance guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq \ker(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y),
\quad
\operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}\cap\mathcal N_{\sing}=0
\text{ with polynomial angle.}
}
\]

---

# 2. \(s_7\)-row nuisance test

The \(s_7\)-row is

\[
\ell_7(u)=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

Thus nuisance is \(s_7\)-dead iff

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

In quotient language:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Allowed nuisance directions are line motions:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}n=\chi(n)A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Forbidden nuisance directions are transverse septic package motions:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}n\notin\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

If such a nuisance exists, then either:

1. the \(s_7\)-row is not actually forbidden, because it is a representation artifact; or
2. \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) is too large and has overcollapsed the D4 obstruction.

Given the D4 construction, case 2 is the expected diagnosis unless a real gauge identity is produced.

So the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f}, Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}]
=1.
}
\]

Together with:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f}, Q_7^{\pkg}C_7]
=2.
}
\]

---

# 3. \(s_9^Y\)-row nuisance test

The \(s_9^Y\)-row is

\[
\ell_9^Y(u)=\omega_Y(P_Wu).
\]

Thus nuisance is \(Y12\)-dead iff

\[
\boxed{
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

Allowed nuisance directions in the order-nine sector include:

\[
DN_0[D^2L],
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2,
\]

transported/edge whitening directions, chart artifacts, and tail terms.

Forbidden nuisance directions include the primitive same-point \(Y12\) whitening carrier:

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

So the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

If nuisance contains \(a_Y\), the \(Y12\)-row is overcollapsed.

---

# 4. Cross-row nuisance relation

Even if nuisance kills neither row separately, it could identify the two rows:

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in \mathcal N_{\sing}+D_{2Y}.
\]

But the two-grade decomposition should prevent this:

\[
e_7\in \mathcal J_7,\qquad e_Y\in \mathcal J_9.
\]

So require:

\[
\boxed{
D_{2Y}=\mathcal D_7\oplus\mathcal D_9
\quad\text{and}\quad
\mathcal N_{\sing}=\mathcal N_7\oplus\mathcal N_9
}
\]

or at least block-triangular with no live \(7\leftrightarrow9\) relation.

Then a relation

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}
\]

implies separately:

\[
e_Y\in \mathcal D_9,
\qquad
\lambda e_7\in \mathcal D_7.
\]

Both are excluded by the row transversality conditions.

Thus grade homogeneity is enough to prevent live-pair collapse.

---

# 5. Nuisance theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NuisanceRowDeadness}_{2Y}.}
\]

Assume:

1. \(Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}\);

2. \(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\);

3. \(\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0\) with polynomial lower bound;

4. \(a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\) with polynomial separation;

5. the nuisance denominator is homogeneous in the order-seven/order-nine decomposition.

Then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

and nuisance preserves the live pair:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

Thus nuisance contributes no column to \(B_Y\).

---

# 6. Failure routing

## A. Nuisance has transverse \(s_7\)

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0.
\]

If genuine gauge, first D4 row is not forbidden.

If not genuine gauge, denominator overcollapsed; refine \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\).

## B. Nuisance has primitive \(Y12\)

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0.
\]

If vertical:

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y,
\]

the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

If graph-like:

\[
(1,\rho_Y)\in B_Y,
\]

rank two collapses to rank one.

If it is pure nuisance overcollapse, refine the quotient.

## C. Nuisance identifies \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\)

Rank two collapses even if each row looks live separately. Enforce two-grade homogeneity.

## D. Poor polynomial angle

Rows exist algebraically but with norm too large. Analytic rank-two estimates fail unless renormalized.

---

# 7. Consequence for local \(B_Y=0\)

If row exactness for canonical actual source holds, no-atom-fitting holds for \(E_2+R_{\lab}\), and NuisanceRowDeadness holds, then

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
D_{\loc}E_2
+
D_{\loc}R_{\lab}
\right]=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then D4 remains rank two with the honest plain wall:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H(c_7\Gamma_G,\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

---

# 8. Remaining exact local blockers

The local proof now has five explicit row-level blockers:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
2.\ \omega_Y(P_WD_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
5.\ \omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0.
}
\]

If all vanish and the live denominators are nonzero with polynomial lower bounds, \(B_Y=0\) is proved.
